1:
1.
Each day I'll do a gold-en deed,
By helping those who are in need;
My life on earth is but a span,
And so I'll do the best I can,
the best I can.
1c:
Life's evening sun is sinking low,
A few more days and I must go,
To meet the deeds that I have done
Where there will be no set-ting sun.
2:
2.
To be a child of God each day,
My light must shine along the way;
I'll sing His praise while ages roll,
And strive to help some troubled soul,
some troubled soul.
2c:
Life's evening sun is sinking low,
A few more days and I must go,
To meet the deeds that I have done
Where there will be no set-ting sun.
3:
3.
The only life that will endure,
Is one that's kind and good and pure;
And so for God I'll take my stand,
Each day I'll lend a helping hand,
a helping hand.
3c:
Life's evening sun is sinking low,
A few more days and I must go,
To meet the deeds that I have done
Where there will be no set-ting sun.
4:
4.
I'll help some one in time of need,
And journey on with rapid speed;
I'll help the sick and poor and weak,
And words of kindness to them speak,
kind words I'll speak.
4c:
Life's evening sun is sinking low,
A few more days and I must go,
To meet the deeds that I have done
Where there will be no set-ting sun.
5:
5.
While going down life's weary road,
I'll try to lift some traveler's load;
I'll try to turn the night to day,
Make flowers bloom along the way,
The lonely way.
5c:
Life's evening sun is sinking low,
A few more days and I must go,
To meet the deeds that I have done
Where there will be no set-ting sun.

001.title: A Beautiful Life
001.credits: Words and Music by: William M. Golden

1:
1.
A charge to keep I have,
A God to glorify;
A never dying soul to save,
And fit it for the sky.
2:
2.
To serve the present age,
My calling to fulfill
O may it all my powers engage
To do my Master's will!
3:
3.
Arm me with jealous care,
As in Thy sight to live,
And O, thy servant, Lord, prepare,
A strict account to give!
4:
4.
Help me to watch and pray,
And on Thyself rely,
Assured if I my trust betray,
I shall forever die.

003.title: A Charge to Keep I Have
003.credits:
Words by: Charles Wesley
Music by: Lowell Mason

1:
1.
A mighty fortress is our God,
A bulwark never failing,
Our helper He, amid the flood
Of mortal ills prevailing;
For still our ancient foe
Doth seek to work us woe;
His craft and power are great,
And armed with cruel hate,
On earth is not his equal.
2:
2.
Did we in our own strength confide,
Our striving would be losing,
Were not the right man on our side,
The man of God's own choosing,
Dost ask who that may be?
Christ Jesus, it is He!
Lord Sabaoth is His name,
From age to age the same,
And He must win the battle.
3:
3.
And though this world, with evil filled,
Should threaten to undo us,
We will not fear, for God hath willed
His truth to triumph thru us,
The prince of darkness grim -
We tremble not for him;
His rage we can endure,
For lo! his doom is sure:
One little word shall fell him.

006.title: A Mighty Fortress
006.credits: Words and Music by: Martin Luther / Arr.: Johann S. Bach

1:
1.
Abide with me: fast falls the even tide;
The darkness deepens: Lord, with me abide!
When other helpers fail, and comforts flee,
Help of the helpless, O abide with me!
3:
3.
I need Thy presence every passing hour:
What but Thy grace can foil the tempter's power?
Who like Thyself my guide and stay can be?
Thru cloud and sunshine, O abide with me!

007.title: Abide with Me
007.credits:
Words: Henry F. Lyte 
Music: William H. Monk

1:
1.
A wonderful Savior is Jesus my Lord,
A wonderful Savior to me;
He hideth my soul in the cleft of the rock,
Where rivers of pleasure I see.
1c:
He hideth my soul in the cleft of the rock
That shadows a dry, thirsty land;
He hideth my life in the depths of His love,
And covers me there with His hand,
And covers me there with His hand.
2:
2.
A wonderful Savior is Jesus my Lord,
He taketh my burden away;
He holdeth me up, and I shall not be moved,
He giveth me strength as my day.
2c:
He hideth my soul in the cleft of the rock
That shadows a dry, thirsty land;
He hideth my life in the depths of His love,
And covers me there with His hand,
And covers me there with His hand.
3:
3.
With numberless blessings each moment He crowns,
And filled with His fullness divine,
I sing in my rapture, O glory to God
For such a Redeemer as mine!
3c:
He hideth my soul in the cleft of the rock
That shadows a dry, thirsty land;
He hideth my life in the depths of His love,
And covers me there with His hand,
And covers me there with His hand.
4:
4.
When clothed in His brightness, transported I rise
To meet Him in clouds of the sky,
His perfect salvation, His wonderful love,
I'll shout with the millions on high.
4c:
He hideth my soul in the cleft of the rock
That shadows a dry, thirsty land;
He hideth my life in the depths of His love,
And covers me there with His hand,
And covers me there with His hand.

009.title: A Wonderful Savior
009.credits:
Words by: Fanny J. Crosby
Music by: William J. Kirkpatrick

esp.title: ¡Oh Que Salvador!
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Fanny J. Crosby
Musica por: William J. Kirkpatrick

1:
1.
Alas! And did my Savior bleed?
And did my Sovereign die?
Would He devote that sacred head
For such a worm as I?
2:
2.
Was it for crimes that I have done
He groaned upon the tree?
Amazing pity! Grace unknown!
And love beyond degree!
3:
3.
Well might the sun in darkness hide,
And shut his glories in,
When Christ, the mighty Maker died
For man, the creature's sin.
4:
4.
Thus might I hide my blushing face
While His dear cross appears;
Dissolve my heart in thankfulness,
an melt mine eyes to tears.
5:
5.
But drops of grief can ne'er repay
The debt of love I owe.
Here, Lord, I give myself away,
"Tis all that I can do!
5c:
At the cross, at the cross where I first saw the light,
And the burden of my heart rolled away,
It was there by faith I received my sight,
And now I am happy all the day.

012.title: Alas! And Did My Savior Bleed
012.credits:
Words by: Isaac Watts, chorus by Ralph E. Hudson
Music by: Ralph E. Hudson

esp.title: En La Cruz
esp.credits: Palabras por: Isaac Watts, coro por: Ralph E. HudsonMusica por: Ralph E. Hudson

1:
1.
Again the Lord of Light and life
Awakes the kindling ray,
Unseals the eyelids of the morn,
And pours increasing day.
2:
2.
O what a night was that which wrapt
The heathen world in gloom!
O what a Sun which rose this day
Triumphant from the tomb!
3:
3.
This day be grateful homage paid,
And loud hosannas sung;
Let gladness dwell in every heart,
And praise on every tongue.
4:
4.
Ten thousand different lips shall join
To hail this welcome morn,
Which scatters blessings from its wings
To nations yet unborn.

013.title: Again the Lord of Light and Life
013.credits:
Words by: Anna L. Barbauld
Music by: Thomas A. Arne

1:
1.
All for Jesus! All for Jesus!
All my being's ransomed powers:
All my thoughts and words and doings,
All my days and all my hours.
All for Jesus! All for Jesus!
All my days and all my hours;
All for Jesus! All for Jesus!
All my days and all my hours.
2:
2.
Let my hands perform His bidding,
Let my feet run in His ways,
Let my eyes see Jesus only,
Let my lips speak forth His praise.
All for Jesus! All for Jesus!
Let my lips speak forth His praise;
All for Jesus! All for Jesus!
Let my lips speak forth His praise.
3:
3.
Since my eyes were fixed on Jesus 
I've lost sight of all beside;
So enchained my spirit's vision,
Looking at the Crucified.
All for Jesus! All for Jesus!
Looking at the Crucified;
All for Jesus! All for Jesus!
Looking at the Crucified.
4:
4.
O what wonder! How amazing!
Jesus glorious King of kings,
Deigns to call me His beloved, 
Bids me rest beneath His wings.
All for Jesus! All for Jesus!
Resting now beneath His wings;
All for Jesus! All for Jesus!
Resting now beneath His wings.

015.title: All for Jesus
015.credits:
Words: Mary Dogworthy James
Music: McDonald’s Songs of Joy & Gladness

2:
2.
The Lord, ye know, is God indeed:
With-out our aid He did us make;
We are His flock, He doth us feed,
And for His sheep He doth us take.
3:
3.
O enter then His gates with praise,
Approach with joy His courts unto;
Praise, laud and bless His name al-ways,
For it is seemly so to do.

017.title: All People That on Earth Do Dwell
017.credits:
Words: Psalm 100 / Arr.: William Kethe
Music: Louis Bourgeois’s Genevan Psalter

esp.title: Gente Que En La Tierra Moran
esp.credits:
Palabras: Psalm 100 / Arr.: William Kethe
Música: Louis Bourgeois’s Genevan Psalter

1:
1.
All hail the power of Jesus' name!  
Let angels prostrate fall!  
Bring forth the royal diadem,
And crown Him Lord of all;  
Bring forth the royal diadem,
And crown Him Lord of all.
2:
2.
Ye chosen seed of Israel's race,
Ye ransomed from the fall,
Hail Him who saves you by His grace,
And crown Him Lord of all;  
Hail Him who saves you by His grace,
And crown Him Lord of all.
3:
3.
Let every kindred, every tribe,
On this terrestrial ball,
To Him all majesty ascribe,
And crown Him Lord of all;  
To Him all majesty ascribe,
And crown Him Lord of all.
4:
4.
O that with yonder sacred throng
We at His feet may fall!  
We'll join the everlasting song,
And crown Him Lord of all;  
We'll join the everlasting song,
And crown Him Lord of all.

019.title: All Hail the Power of Jesus’ Name
019.credits:
Words: Edward Perronet, St. 4, John Rippon
Music: Oliver Holden

esp.title: Load El Nombre De Jesús
esp.credits: Palabras por: Edward Perronet, St. 4, John RipponMusica por: Oliver Holden

1:
1.
All the way my Savior leads me:
What have I to ask beside?
Can I doubt His tender mercy,
Who through life has been my Guide?
Heavenly peace, divinest comfort,
Here by faith in Him to dwell!
For I know, whatever befall me,
Jesus doeth all things well;
For I know, whatever befall me,
Jesus do-eth all things well.
2:
2.
All the way my Savior leads me,
Cheers each winding path I tread,
Gives me grace for every trial,
Feeds me with the living bread;
Though my weary steps may falter,
And my soul athirst may be,
Gushing from the Rock before me,
Lo! A spring of joy I see;
Gushing from the Rock before me,
Lo! A spring of joy I see.
3:
3.
All the way my Savior leads me:
O the fullness of His love!
Perfect rest to me is promised 
In my Father's house above;
When my spirit, clothed immortal,
Wings its flight to realms of day,
This my song through endless ages:
Jesus led me all the way!
This my song through endless ages:
Jesus led me all the way!

022.title: All the Way My Savior Leads Me
022.credits:
Words: Fanny J. Crosby
Music: Robert Lowry

esp.title: Cristo Es Guía De Me Vida
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Fanny J. Crosby
Musica por: Robert Lowry

1:
1.
All things praise Thee, Lord most high,
Heaven and earth, and sea and sky;
All were for Thy glory made,
That Thy greatness, thus displayed,
Should all worship bring to Thee;
All things praise Thee— Lord, may we!
3:
3.
All things praise Thee: heaven's high shrine
Rings with melody divine;
Lowly bending at Thy feet,
Seraph and archangel meet;
This their highest bliss, to be
Ever praising— Lord, may we!

025.title: All Things Praise Thee
025.credits:
Words: George W. Conder
Music: Conrad Kocher / Arr.: William H. Monk

esp.title: Todo Te Alaba
esp.credits:
Palabras: George W. Conder
Musica: Conrad Kocher / Arr.: William H. Monk

1:
1.
All to Jesus I surrender,
All to Him I freely give;
I will ever love and trust Him,
In His presence daily live.
1c:
I surrender all,
I surrender all;
All to Thee, my bless-ed Savior,
I surrender all.
2:
2.
All to Jesus I surrender,
Humbly at His feet I bow;
Worldly pleasures all forsaken,
Take me, Jesus, take me now.
2c:
I surrender all,
I surrender all;
All to Thee, my bless-ed Savior,
I surrender all.
3:
3.
All to Jesus I surrender,
Lord, I give myself to Thee;
Fill me with Thy love and power,
Let Thy blessing fall on me.
3c:
I surrender all,
I surrender all;
All to Thee, my bless-ed Savior,
I surrender all.

029.title: All to Jesus I Surrender
029.credits:
Words by: J. W. Van De Venter
Music by: W. S. Weeden

esp.title: Todo A Jesús Yo Le Rindo
esp.credits:
Palabras por: J. W. Van De Venter
Música por: W. S. Weeden

2:
2.
"Almost persuaded," come, come, today;
"Almost persuaded," turn not away;
Jesus invites you here,
Angels are lingering near,
Prayers rise from hearts so dear,
O wanderer, come.
3:
3.
"Almost persuaded," harvest is past!
"Almost persuaded," doom comes at last!
"Almost" cannot avail;
"Almost" is but to fail;
Sad, sad, that bitter wail—
"Almost— but lost!"

1:
1.
Amazing grace, how sweet the sound,
That saved a wretch like me!
I once was lost but now am found,
Was blind, but now I see.
2:
2.
‘Twas grace that taught my heart to fear
And grace my fears relieved;
How precious did that grace appear,
The hour I first believed.
3:
3.
Through many dangers, toils, and snares
I have already come;
'Tis grace hath brought me safe thus far,
And grace will lead me home.
4:
4.
The Lord has promised good to me:
His Word my hope secures;
He will my shield and portion be
As long as life endures.
5:
5.
And when this flesh and heart shall fail,
And mortal life shall cease,
I shall possess within the veil
A life of joy and peace.
6:
6.
When we've been there ten thousand years,
Bright, shining as the sun,
We've no less days to sing God's praise
Than when we've first begun.

036.title: Amazing Grace
036.credits:
Words by: John Newton
Music by: Shaw’s & Spilman’s Columbian Harmony / Arr. Edwin O. Excell

1:
1.
Angry words! O let them never
From the tongue unbridled slip;
May the heart's best impulse ever
Check them ere they soil the lip.
2:
2.
Love is much too pure and holy,
Friendship is too sacred far,
For a moment's reckless folly
Thus to desolate and mar.
3:
3.
Angry words are lightly spoken, 
Bitterest thoughts are rashly stirred,
Brightest links of life are broken
By a single angry word.

037.title: Angry Words
037.credits:
Words by: Sunday School Teacher
Music by: Horatio R. Palmer / Arr.: Will W. Slater

esp.title: Palabras Enojadas
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Sunday School Teacher
Musica por: Horatio R. Palmer / Arr.: Will W. Slater

1:
1.
Be with me, Lord I cannot live without Thee,
I dare not try to take one step alone,
I cannot bear the loads of life, unaided,
I need Thy strength to lean myself upon.
2:
2.
Be with me, Lord, and then if dangers threaten,
If storms of trial burst above my head,
If lashing seas leap everywhere about me,
They cannot harm, or make my heart afraid.
3:
3.
Be with me, Lord! No other gift or blessing
Thou couldst bestow could with this one compare
A constant sense of Thy abiding presence,
Where'er I am, to feel that Thou art near.
4:
4.
Be with me, Lord, when loveliness o'ertakes me,
When I must weep amid the fires of pain,
And when shall come the hour of "my departure" 
For "worlds unknown," O Lord, be with me then.

040.title: Be with Me, Lord
040.credits:
Words: T. O. Chisholm / Music: L. O. Sanderson
Copyright © 1963, Renewal, Leon B. Sanderson, owner. 
All Rights Reserved. Used by Permission

3:
3.
Anywhere with Jesus, over land and sea,
Telling souls in darkness of salvation free;
Ready as He summons me to go or stay,
Anywhere with Jesus when He points the way.
4:
4.
Anywhere with Jesus I can go to sleep,
When the darkening shadows round a-bout me creep,
Knowing I shall waken never more to roam;
Anywhere with Jesus will be home, sweet home.

048.title: Anywhere with Jesus
048.credits:
Words by: v1,2,4 Jessie Brown Pounds
Music by: D. B. Towner

esp.title: Dondequiera Con Jesús
esp.credits:
Palabras por: v1,2,4 Jessie Brown Pounds
Musica por: D. B. Towner

1:
1.
Have you been to Jesus for the cleansing power?  
Are you washed in the blood of the Lamb?  
Are you fully trusting in His grace this hour?  
Are you washed in the blood of the Lamb?
1c:
Are you washed in the blood,
In the soul cleansing blood of the Lamb?  
Are your garments spotless?  
Are they white as snow?  
Are you washed in the blood of the Lamb?
2:
2.
Are you walking daily by the Savior's side?  
Are you washed in the blood of the Lamb?  
Do you rest each moment in the Crucified?  
Are you washed in the blood of the Lamb?
2c:
Are you washed in the blood,
In the soul cleansing blood of the Lamb?  
Are your garments spotless?  
Are they white as snow?  
Are you washed in the blood of the Lamb?
3:
3.
When the Bridegroom cometh will your robes be white,
Pure and white in the blood of the Lamb?  
Will your soul be ready for the mansions bright,
And be washed in the blood of the Lamb?
3c:
Are you washed in the blood,
In the soul cleansing blood of the Lamb?  
Are your garments spotless?  
Are they white as snow?  
Are you washed in the blood of the Lamb?
4:
4.
Lay aside the garments that are stained with sin,
And be washed in the blood of the Lamb?  
There's a fountain flowing for the soul unclean:
O be washed in the blood of the Lamb?
4c:
Are you washed in the blood,
In the soul cleansing blood of the Lamb?  
Are your garments spotless?  
Are they white as snow?  
Are you washed in the blood of the Lamb?

050.title: Are You Washed in the Blood?
050.credits: Words and Music: E. A. Hoffman

esp.title: ¿Eres Limpio En La Sangre?
esp.credits: Palabras y Musica: E. A. Hoffman

1:
1.
All praise to Him who reigns above,
In majesty supreme;  
Who gave His Son for man to die,
That He might man redeem.
1c:
Blessed be the name, blessed be the name,
Blessed be the name of the Lord;  
Blessed be the name, blessed be the name,
Blessed be the name of the Lord.
2:
2.
His name above all names shall stand,
Exalted more and more,
At God the Father's own right hand
Where angel hosts adore.
2c:
Blessed be the name, blessed be the name,
Blessed be the name of the Lord;  
Blessed be the name, blessed be the name,
Blessed be the name of the Lord.
3:
3.
Redeemer, Savior, Friend of man
Once ruined by the fall;  
Thou hast devised salvation's plan,
For Thou hast died for all.
3c:
Blessed be the name, blessed be the name,
Blessed be the name of the Lord;  
Blessed be the name, blessed be the name,
Blessed be the name of the Lord.

052.title: Blessed Be the Name
052.credits:
Words: W. H. Clark / Refrain: Ralph E. Hudson
Music: Ralph E. Hudson / Arr. William J. Kirkpatrick

1:
1.
Years I spent in vanity and pride,
Caring not my Lord was crucified,
Knowing not it was for me He died
On Calvary.
2:
2.
By God's word at last my sin I learned;  
Then I trembled at the law I'd spurned,
Till my guilty soul imploring turned
To Calvary.
4:
4.
O the love that drew salvation's plan!  
O the grace that brought it down to man!  
O the mighty gulf that God did span
At Calvary.
4c: End

053.title: At Calvary
053.credits:
Words by: William R. Newell
Music by: Daniel B. Towner

esp.title: Años Mi Alma En Vanidad Vivió
esp.credits:
Palabras por: William R. Newell
Música por: Daniel B. Towner

1:
1.
Beyond this land of parting, losing and leaving,
Far beyond the losses darkening this,
And far beyond the taking and the bereaving,
Lies the summer-land of bliss.
1c:
Land beyond, so fair and bright!
Land beyond where is no night!
Summer-land, God is its Light,
O happy summer-land of bliss!
2:
2.
Beyond this land of toiling, sowing and reaping,
Far beyond the shadows darkening this,
And far beyond the sighing, moaning and weeping,
Lies the summer-land of bliss.
2c:
Land beyond, so fair and bright!
Land beyond where is no night!
Summer-land, God is its Light,
O happy summer-land of bliss!
3:
3.
Beyond this land of waiting, seeking and sighing,
Far beyond the sorrows darkening this,
And far beyond the pain and sickness and dying
Lies the summer-land of bliss.
3c:
Land beyond, so fair and bright!
Land beyond where is no night!
Summer-land, God is its Light,
O happy summer-land of bliss!
End

055.title: Beyond This Land of Parting
055.credits:
Words: Mary B. C. Slade
Music: Asa B. Everett

esp.title: Más Allá De La Tierra De Partidas
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Mary B. C. Slade
Musica por: Asa B. Everett

1:
1.
Break Thou the bread of life,
Dear Lord to me,
As Thou didst break the loaves
Beside the sea;  
Beyond the sacred page
I seek Thee, Lord;  
My spirit pants for Thee,
O living Word!
2:
2.
Bless Thou the truth, dear Lord,
To me, to me,
As thou didst bless the bread
By Galilee;  
Then shall all bondage cease,
All fetters fall,
And I shall find my peace,
My All in all.

059.title: Break Thou the Bread of Life
059.credits:
Words by: Mary Artemisia Lathbury
Music by: William F. Sherwin

esp.title: Parte El Pan De Vida
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Mary Artemisia Lathbury
Música por: William F. Sherwin

1:
1.
The voice of the Savior says "Come;"
The cross where He died is in sight;
Even now at the cross there is room:
Are you coming to Jesus tonight?
1c:
Are you coming to Jesus tonight?
Are you coming to Jesus tonight?
The Bride and the Spirit invite:
Are you coming to Jesus tonight?
2:
2.
The voice of the Father implores
From mercy's most wonderful height;
His love in that call He outpours:
Are you coming to Jesus tonight?
2c:
Are you coming to Jesus tonight?
Are you coming to Jesus tonight?
The Bride and the Spirit invite:
Are you coming to Jesus tonight?
3:
3.
The voices of loved ones entreat:
You know in your heart they are right;
Then list, for the moments are fleet!
Are you coming to Jesus tonight?
3c:
Are you coming to Jesus tonight?
Are you coming to Jesus tonight?
The Bride and the Spirit invite:
Are you coming to Jesus tonight?
4:
4.
The voices of friends gone before
Seem floating from regions of light;
They tenderly say over and over,
Are you coming to Jesus tonight?
4c:
Are you coming to Jesus tonight?
Are you coming to Jesus tonight?
The Bride and the Spirit invite:
Are you coming to Jesus tonight?
5:
5.
O who to himself will be true,
Of all whom these voices invite?
Who answers, my brother do you?
Are you coming to Jesus tonight?
5c:
Are you coming to Jesus tonight?
Are you coming to Jesus tonight?
The Bride and the Spirit invite:
Are you coming to Jesus tonight?

061.title: Are You Coming to Jesus Tonight?
061.credits:
Words: Jessie Brown Pounds
Music: James E. Hawes

2:
2.
His sovereign power, without our aid,
Made us of clay, and formed us men;
And when like wandering sheep we strayed,
He brought us to His fold again.
3:
3.
We'll crowd Thy gates with thankful songs,
High as the heavens our voices raise;
And earth, with her ten thousand tongues,
Shall fill Thy courts with sounding praise.
4:
4.
Wide as the world is Thy command,
Vast as eternity Thy love;
Firm as a rock Thy truth shall stand
When rolling years shall cease to move.

064.title: Before Jehovah’s Awful Throne
064.credits:
Words: Isaac Watts / alt.: Charles Wesley
Music: Louis Bourgeois

1:
1.
Behold a stranger at the door!
He gently knocks, has knocked before,
Has waited long, is waiting still;
You treat no other friend so ill.
2:
2.
But will He prove a Friend indeed?
He will— the very Friend you need!
The Man of Nazareth, 'tis He,
With garments dyed at Calvary.
3:
3.
O lovely attitude! He stands
With melting heart and laden hands!
O matchless kindness! And He shows
This matchless kindness to His foes.
4:
4.
Admit Him, for the human breast
Never entertained so kind a guest;
No mortal tongue their joys can tell
With whom He condescends to dwell.

065.title: Behold a Stranger at the Door
065.credits:
Words; Joseph Grigg
Music: George Hews

1:
1.
Bring Christ your broken life,
So marred by sin,
He will create anew, 
Make whole again;
Your empty, wasted years
He will restore,
And your iniquities
Remember no more.
2:
2.
Bring Him your every care
If great or small -
Whatever troubles you -
O bring it all!
Bring Him the haunting fears,
The nameless dread,
Thy heart He will relieve,
And lift up thy head.
3:
3.
Bring Him your weariness, 
Receive His rest;
Weep out your blinding tears
Upon His breast;
His love is wonderful,
His power is great,
"And none that trust in Him
Shall be desolate."
4:
4.
Blest Savior of us all!
Almighty Friend!
His presence shall be ours
Unto the end;
Without Him life would be
How dark, how drear!
But with Him morning breaks -
And heaven is near!

067.title: Bring Christ Your Broken Life
067.credits:
Words: Thomas O. Chisholm / Music: Lloyd O. Sanderson
Copyright © 1935, Renewed 1963 by L. O. Sanderson, Leon B. Sanderson, Owner.
All rights reserved. Used by permission.

esp.title: Trae Tu Vida A Cristo
esp.credits:
Palabras: Thomas O. Chisholm / Música: Lloyd O. Sanderson
Los Derechos De Autor © 1935, Renovado 1963 por L. O. Sanderson, Leon B. Sanderson, Dueño.
Todos Los Derechos Reservados.Utilizado Por Permiso.

1:
1.
God sent His Son, they called Him Jesus,
He came to love, heal and forgive;
He lived and died to buy my pardon,
An empty grave is there to prove my Savior lives.
1c:
Because He lives I can face tomorrow,
Because He lives all fear is gone;
Because I know He holds the future,
And life is worth the living just because He lives.
2:
2.
How sweet to hold a newborn baby,
And feel the pride and joy he gives;
But greater still, the calm assurance,
This child can face uncertain days because He lives.
2c:
Because He lives I can face tomorrow,
Because He lives all fear is gone;
Because I know He holds the future,
And life is worth the living just because He lives.
3:
3.
And then one day I'll cross the river,
I'll fight life's final war with pain;
And then as death gives way to victory,
I'll see the lights of glory and I'll know He lives.
3c:
Because He lives I can face tomorrow,
Because He lives all fear is gone;
Because I know He holds the future,
And life is worth the living just because He lives.

068.title: Because He Lives
068.credits:
Words by: William J. Gaither & Gloria Gaither
Music by: William J. Gaither
Copyright © 1971 by William J. Gaither (ASCAP) / All rights reserved / Used by permission.

esp.title: Porque Él Vive
esp.credits:
Palabras por: William J. Gaither & Gloria Gaither
Música por: William J. Gaither
Los Derechos De Autor © 1971 por William J. Gaither (ASCAP) / Todos Los Derechos Reservados / Utilizado Por El Permiso.

1:
1.
Be Thou my vision, O Lord of my heart;
Naught be all else to me save that Thou art,
Thou my best thought, by day or by night,
Waking or sleeping, Thy presence my light.
4:
4.
Riches I heed not, nor man's empty praise;
Thou my inheritance, now and always:
Thou and Thou only, first in my heart,
High King of heaven, my treasure Thou art.
5:
5.
High King of heaven, when victory is won
May I reach heaven's joys, O bright heaven's sun!
Heart of my heart, whatever be-fall,
Still be my vision, O ruler of all.

070.title: Be Thou My Vision
070.credits:
Words: Irish Folk Hymn
Music: Joyce’s Old Irish Folk Music, Arr: Martin Shaw

esp.title: Oh Dios Mi Alma
esp.credits:
Palabras: Irish Folk Hymn
Musica: Joyce’s Old Irish Folk Music, Arr: Martin Shaw

1:
1.
Blessed assurance, Jesus is mine!  
O what a foretaste of glory divine!  
Heir of salvation, purchase of God,
Born of His Spirit, washed in His blood.
1c:
This is my story, this is my song,
Praising my Savior all the day long;  
This is my story, this is my song,
Praising my Savior all the day long.
2:
2.
Perfect submission, perfect delight,
Visions of rapture now burst on my sight;  
Angels descending bring from above
Echoes of mercy, whispers of love.

071.title: Blessed Assurance
071.credits:
Words by: Fanny J. Crosby
Music by: Mrs. Joseph F. Knapp

esp.title: Grata Certeza
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Fanny J. Crosby
Musica por: Mrs. Joseph F. Knapp

1:
1.
Blest be the dear uniting love
That will not let us part:
Our bodies may far off remove,
We still are one in heart.
2:
2.
Joined in one Spirit to our Head,
Where He appoints we go,
And still in Jesus' footsteps tread,
And show His praise below.
3:
3.
O may we ever walk in Him,
And nothing know beside,
Nothing desire, nothing esteem,
But Jesus crucified.
4:
4.
Closer and closer let us cleave
To His beloved embrace,
Expect His fullness to receive,
And grace to answer grace.

074.title: Blest Be the Dear, Uniting Love
074.credits:
Words: Charles Wesley
Music: Ephraim Timothy Hildebrand

1:
1.
Blest be the tie that binds
Our hearts in Christian love;  
The fellow-ship of kindred minds
Is like to that above.
2:
2.
Before our Father's throne,
We pour our ardent prayers;  
Our fears, our hopes, our aims are one,
Our com-forts and our cares.
4:
4.
When we asunder part,
It gives us inward pain;  
But we shall still be joined in heart,
And hope to meet again.
5:
5.
This glorious hope revives
Our courage by the way;
While each in expectation lives,
And longs to see the day.
6:
6.
From sorrow, toil, and pain,
And sin, we shall be free;
And perfect love and friendship reign
Thro' all eternity.

076.title: Blest Be the Tie That Binds
076.credits:
Words by: John Fawcett
Music by: Johann George Nägeli / Arr.: Lowell Mason

esp.title: Sagrado Es El Amor
esp.credits:
Palabras por: John Fawcett
Musica por: Johann George Nägeli / Arr.: Lowell Mason

1:
1.
Beneath the cross of Jesus
I fain would take my stand,
The shadow of a mighty rock
Within a weary land,
A home within the wilderness,
A rest upon the way,
From the burning of the noontide heat,
And the bur-den of the day.
2:
2.
O safe and happy shelter,
O refuge tried and sweet,
O trysting place where heaven's love
And heaven's justice meet!  
As to the holy patriarch
That wondrous dream was given, 
So seems my Savior's cross to me,
A ladder up to heaven.
3:
3.
Upon that cross of Jesus,
Mine eye at times can see
The very dying form of One
Who suffered there for me;  
And from my smitten heart, with tears
Two wonders I confess;  
The wonders of His glorious love,
And my own worth-less-ness.
4:
4.
I take, O cross, thy shadow
For my abiding place;  
I ask no other sunshine than
The sunshine of His face;  
Content to let the world go by,
To know no gain nor loss,
My sinful self my only shame,
My glory all the cross!

080.title: Beneath the Cross of Jesus
080.credits:
Words by: Elizabeth Clephane
Music by: Frederick C. Maker

esp.title: Bajo La Cruz De Jesús
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Elizabeth Clephane
Musica por: Frederick C. Maker

1:
1.
Buried with Christ, my blessed Redeemer,
Dead to the old life of folly and sin;
Sa-tan may call, the world may en-treat me,
There is no voice that answers with-in.
1c:
Dead to the world, to voices that call me,
Living anew, obedient but free;
Dead to the joys that once did enthrall me
Yet 'tis not I, Christ liveth in me.
2:
2.
Think it not strange that things I once cherished
Cannot allure me or charm as before;
For in the flesh with Christ I have suffered,
Old things are passed, I love them no more.
2c:
Dead to the world, to voices that call me,
Living anew, obedient but free;
Dead to the joys that once did enthrall me
Yet 'tis not I, Christ liveth in me.
3:
3.
Dead unto sin, alive through the Spirit,
Risen with Him from the gloom of the grave,
All things are new, and I am rejoicing
In His great love, his power to save.
3c:
Dead to the world, to voices that call me,
Living anew, obedient but free;
Dead to the joys that once did enthrall me
Yet 'tis not I, Christ liveth in me.
4:
4.
Sin hath no more its cruel dominion,
Walking "in new-ness of life," I am free
Glorious life of Christ, my Redeemer,
Which He so richly shareth with me.
4c:
Dead to the world, to voices that call me,
Living anew, obedient but free;
Dead to the joys that once did enthrall me
Yet 'tis not I, Christ liveth in me.
End

085.title: Buried with Christ
085.credits:
Words by: T. O. Chisholm / Music by: L. O. Sanderson
Copyright © 1963, Renewal, Leon B. Sanderson, Owner. 
All rights reserved. Used by permission.

esp.title: Sepultado Estoy Con Mi Cristo
esp.credits:
Palabras por: T. O. Chisholm / Música por: L. O. Sanderson
Los Derechos De Autor © 1963, Renewal, Leon B. Sanderson, Dueño. 
Todos Los Derechos Reservados. Utilizado Por El Permiso.

1:
1.
By Christ redeemed, in Christ restored,
We keep the memory adored,
And show the death of our dear Lord,
Until He come.
2:
2.
His body given in our stead
Is seen in this memorial bread,
And so our feeble love is fed,
Until He come.
3:
3.
His fearful unknown agony,
His life-blood shed for us we see;
The wine shall tell the mystery,
Until He come.
4:
4.
And thus that dark betrayal night
With the last ad-vent we unite,
By one bright chain of loving rite,
Until He come.

086.title: By Christ Redeemed
086.credits:
Words by: George Rawson
Music by: from Arthur H. Troyte’s Chant

esp.title: Cristo Me Compro
esp.credits:
Palabras por: George Rawson
Musica por: from Arthur H. Troyte’s Chant

090.title: Christ, We Do All Adore Thee
090.credits:
Words by: Theodore Baker
Music by: Theodore Dubois

esp.title: Cristo Te Adoramos
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Theodore Baker
Musica por: Theodore Dubois

1:
1.
Christ for the world! we sing;
The world to Christ we bring,
With loving zeal;
The poor and them that mourn,
The faint and overborne,
Sin-sick and sorrow-worn,
Whom Christ doth heal.
2:
2.
Christ for the world! we sing;
The world to Christ we bring,
With fervent prayer;
The wayward and the lost,
By restless passions tossed,
Redeemed at countless cost
From dark despair.
3:
3.
Christ for the world! we sing;
The world to Christ we bring,
With one accord,
With us the work to share,
With us reproach to dare,
With us the cross to bear,
For Christ our Lord.
4:
4.
Christ for the world! we sing;
The world to Christ we bring,
With joyful song;
The newborn souls whose days,
Reclaimed from error's ways,
Inspired with hope and praise,
To Christ belong.

093.title: Christ for the World We Sing
093.credits:
Words by: Samuel Wolcott
Music by: Felice De Giardini

esp.title: Cristo Al Mundo, Cantad
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Samuel Wolcott
Música por: Felice De Giardini

1:
1.
Come, Thou almighty  King,
Help us Thy name to sing,
Help us to praise!
Father all glorious,
O'er all victorious,
Come and reign over us,
Ancient of Days!
2:
2.
Come, Thou Incarnate Word,
Gird on Thy mighty sword,
Our prayer attend!
Come, and Thy people bless,
And give Thy word success:
Spirit of Holiness, 
On us descend!
3:
3.
O Lord, our God, to Thee
The highest praises be,
Hence, evermore;
Thy sovereign majesty 
May we in glory see,
And to eternity
Love and adore!

100.title: Come, Thou Almighty King
100.credits:
Words by: Whitfield’s Collection of Hymns
Music by: Felice De Giardini

1:
1.
Come to Jesus! He will save you,
Though your sins as crimson glow;
If you give your heart to Jesus,
He will make it white as snow.
1c:
Come to Jesus!
Come to Jesus!
Come to Jesus! Come today!
Come to Jesus!
Come to Jesus!
Come to Jesus! Come, come today!
2:
2.
Come to Jesus! do not tarry,
Enter in at mercy's gate;
Oh, delay not till the morrow,
Lest thy coming be too late.
2c:
Come to Jesus!
Come to Jesus!
Come to Jesus! Come today!
Come to Jesus!
Come to Jesus!
Come to Jesus! Come, come today!
3:
3.
Come to Jesus, dying sinner!
Other Savior there is none;
He will share with you His glory,
When your pilgrim-age is done.
3c:
Come to Jesus!
Come to Jesus!
Come to Jesus! Come today!
Come to Jesus!
Come to Jesus!
Come to Jesus! Come, come today!

103.title: Come To Jesus
103.credits:
Words: Eden Reeder Latta
Music: John Harrison Tenney

1:
1.
O heart bowed down with sorrow!
O eyes that long for sight!
There's gladness in believing,
In Jesus there is light.
1c:
"Come unto me,
All ye that labor
And are heavy laden,
And I will give you rest.
Take My yoke upon you,
And learn of Me;
For I am meek and lowly in heart:
And ye shall find rest unto your souls."
2:
2.
Divinest consolation 
Doth Christ the Healer give;
Art thou in condemnation?
Believe, repent and live.
2c:
"Come unto me,
All ye that labor
And are heavy laden,
And I will give you rest.
Take My yoke upon you,
And learn of Me;
For I am meek and lowly in heart:
And ye shall find rest unto your souls."
3c:
"Come unto me,
All ye that labor
And are heavy laden,
And I will give you rest.
Take My yoke upon you,
And learn of Me;
For I am meek and lowly in heart:
And ye shall find rest unto your souls."

107.title: Come unto Me
107.credits:
Words & Music: Franklin E. Belden / Arr.: Ellis J. Crum
Arr. © 1959 in Sacred Selections for the Church. Renewal 1987 by Ellis J. Crum, Kendallville, IN 46755
All rights reserved. International copyright secured. Used by permission.

1:
1.
Come, Thou long expected Jesus,
Born to set Thy people free.
From our fears and sins release us;
Let us find our rest in Thee.
Israel's Strength and Consolation,
Hope of all the earth Thou art;
Dear Desire of every nation,
Joy of every longing heart!
2:
2.
Born Thy people to deliver,
Born a child and yet a King,
Born to reign in us forever,
Now Thy gracious kingdom bring.
By Thine own eternal Spirit
Rule in all our hearts alone;
By Thine all sufficient merit,
Raise us to Thy glorious throne.

108.title: Come, Thou Long Expected Jesus
108.credits:
Words: Charles Wesley
Music: Rowland H. Prichard, Arr: Ralph Vaughn Williams

1:
1.
Come, we that love the Lord,
And let our joys be known;
Join in a song with sweet accord,
Join in a song with sweet accord,
And thus surround the throne,
And thus surround the throne.
1c:
We're marching to Zion,
Beautiful, beautiful Zion;
We're marching upward to Zion,
The beautiful city of God.
2:
2.
Let those refuse to sing
Who never knew our God;
But children of the heavenly King,
But children of the heavenly King,
May speak their joys abroad,
May speak their joys abroad.
2c:
We're marching to Zion,
Beautiful, beautiful Zion;
We're marching upward to Zion,
The beautiful city of God.
3:
3.
The hill of Zion yields
A thousand sacred sweets,
Before we reach the heavenly fields,
Before we reach the heavenly fields,
Or walk the golden streets,
Or walk the golden streets.
3c:
We're marching to Zion,
Beautiful, beautiful Zion;
We're marching upward to Zion,
The beautiful city of God.
4:
4.
Then let our songs abound,
And every tear be dry;
We're marching through Immanuel's ground,
We're marching through Immanuel's ground,
To fairer worlds on high,
To fairer worlds on high.
4c:
We're marching to Zion,
Beautiful, beautiful Zion;
We're marching upward to Zion,
The beautiful city of God.

111.title: Come, We That Love the Lord
111.credits:
Words by: Isaac Watts / Ref.: Robert Lowry
Music by: Robert Lowry

esp.title: Los Que Aman Al Señor
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Isaac Watts / Ref.: Robert Lowry
Música por: Robert Lowry

1:
1.
Crown Him with many crowns, 
The Lamb upon His throne;
Hark, how the heavenly anthem drowns
All music but its own!
Awake, my soul, and sing
Of Him who died for thee,
And hail Him as thy matchless King
Through all eternity.
2:
2.
Crown Him the Lord of life,
Who triumphed o'er the grave,
Who rose victorious in the strife
For those He came to save!
His glories now we sing,
Who died and rose on high,
Who died eternal life to bring,
And lives that death may die.
3:
3.
Crown Him the Lord of peace,
Whose power a scepter sways
From pole to pole, that wars may cease,
Absorbed in prayer and praise:
His reign shall know no end,
And round His pierced feet
Fair flowers of Paradise extend,
Their fragrance ever sweet.
4:
4.
Crown Him the Lord of heaven,
One with the Father known,
And the blest Spirit, through Him given
From yonder glorious throne!
All hail, Redeemer, hail!
For Thou hast died for me;
Thy praise and glory shall not fail
Throughout eternity.

115.title: Crown Him with Many Crowns
115.credits:
Words by: Matthew Bridges, st.2: Godfrey Thring
Music by: George J. Elvey

esp.title: A Cristo Coronad
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Matthew Bridges, st.2: Godfrey Thring
Musica por: George J. Elvey

1:
1.
When upon life's billows you are tempest-tossed,
When you are discouraged, thinking all is lost,
Count your many blessings, name them one by one,
And it will surprise you what the Lord hath done.
2:
2.
Are you ever burdened with a load of care?
Does the cross seem heavy you are called to bear?
Count your many blessings, every doubt will fly,
And you will be singing as the days go by.
3:
3.
When you look at others with their lands and gold,
Think that Christ has promised you His wealth untold;
Count your many blessings, money cannot buy
Your re-ward in Heaven, nor your home on high.
4:
4.
So, amid the conflict, whether great or small,
Do not be discouraged, God is over all;
Count your many blessings, angels will attend,
Help and comfort give you to your journey's end.

118.title: Count Your Blessings
118.credits:
Words: Johnson Oatman, Jr.
Music: Edwin O. Excell

1:
1.
Day by day and with each passing moment,
Strength I find to meet my trials here;
Trusting in my Father's wise bestowment,
I've no cause for worry or for fear.
He whose heart is kind beyond all measure
Gives unto each day what He deems best—
Lovingly, its part of pain and pleasure,
Mingling toil with peace and rest.
2:
2.
Every day the Lord Himself is near me,
With a special mercy for each hour;
All my cares He fain would bear, and cheer me,
He whose name is Counselor and Power.
The protection of His child and treasure
Is a charge that on Himself He laid:
"As thy days, thy strength shall be in measure,"
This the pledge to me He made.
3:
3.
Help me then in every tribulation
So to trust Thy promises, O Lord,
That I lose not faith's sweet consolation
Offered me within Thy holy Word.
Help me, Lord, when toil and trouble meeting,
Ever to take, as from a father's hand,
One by one, the days, the moments fleeting,
Till I reach the promised land.

120.title: Day by Day
120.credits:
Words: Carolina Sandell Berg / tr. Andrew L. Skoog
Music: Oscar Ahnfelt

esp.title: Cada Día
esp.credits:
Palabras: Carolina Sandell Berg / tr. Andrew L. Skoog
Música: Oscar Ahnfelt

1:
1.
Dear Lord and Father of mankind,
Forgive our foolish ways;
Reclothe us in our rightful mind,
In purer lives Thy service find,
In deeper reverence, praise. s
2:
2.
In simple trust like theirs who heard,
Be-side the Syrian sea,
The gracious calling of the Lord,
Let us, like them, without a word,
Rise up and follow Thee.
3:
3.
O Sabbath rest by Galilee,
O calm of hills above,
Where Jesus knelt to share with thee
The silence of eternity,
Interpreted by love!
4:
4.
Drop thy still dews of quietness,
Till all our strivings cease;
Take from our souls the strain and stress,
And let our ordered lives confess
The beauty of thy peace.
A-men.

122.title: Dear Lord and Father of Mankind
122.credits:
Words by: John G. Whittier
Music by: Frederick C. Maker

1:
1.
Ere you left your room this morning,
Did you think to pray?
In the name of Christ our Savior,
Did you sue for loving favor,
As a shield today?
1c:
O how praying rests the weary!
Prayer will change the night to day;
So when life seems dark and dreary,
Don't forget to pray.
2:
2.
When you met with great temptation,
Did you think to pray?
By His dying love and merit,
Did you claim the Holy Spirit
As your guide and stay?
2c:
O how praying rests the weary!
Prayer will change the night to day;
So when life seems dark and dreary,
Don't forget to pray.
3:
3.
When your heart was filled with anger,
Did you think to pray?
Did you plead for grace, my brother,
That you might forgive another
Who had crossed your way?
3c:
O how praying rests the weary!
Prayer will change the night to day;
So when life seems dark and dreary,
Don't forget to pray.
4:
4.
When sore trials came upon you,
Did you think to pray?
When your soul was bowed in sorrow,
Balm of Gilead did you borrow
At the gates of day?
4c:
O how praying rests the weary!
Prayer will change the night to day;
So when life seems dark and dreary,
Don't forget to pray.

124.title: Did You Think to Pray?
124.credits:
Words: Mary A. Kidder
Music: William O. Perkins

esp.title: ¿Pensastes A Orar?
esp.credits:
Palabras: Mary A. Kidder
Música: William O. Perkins

1:
1. 
Have you a heart that's weary, 
Tending a load of care:
Are you a soul that's seeking
Rest from the bur-den you bear?
1c:
Do you know my Jesus, 
Do you know my friend?
Have you heard He loves you, 
And that He will abide 'till the end?
2:
2. 
Where is you heart, O pilgrim,
What does your light reveal;
Who hears your call for comfort 
When naught but sorrow you feel?
2c:
Do you know my Jesus, 
Do you know my friend?
Have you heard He loves you, 
And that He will abide 'till the end?
3:
3. 
Who knows your disappointments,
Who hears each time you cry;
Who understands your heartaches,
Who dries the tears from your eyes?
3c:
Do you know my Jesus, 
Do you know my friend?
Have you heard He loves you, 
And that He will abide 'till the end?

125.title: Do You Know My Jesus?
125.credits:
Words and Music by: William F. Lakey and V. B. Ellis
Copyright © 1957 by Lillenas Publishing Company (Administered by The Copyright Company Nashville, TN)
All Rights Reserved. International Copyright Secured. Used By Permission.

esp.title: ¿Conoces A Jesús?
esp.credits:
Palabras y Música por: William F. Lakey and V. B. Ellis
Los Derechos De Autor © 1957 por Lillenas Publishing Company (Administered by The Copyright Company Nashville, TN)
Todos Los Derechos Reservados. Derechos De Autor Internacionales Garantizados. Utilizado Por El Permiso.

1:
1.
Each step I take my Savior goes before me,
And with His loving hand He leads the way.
And with each breath I whisper "I adore Thee;"
O what joy to walk with Him each day.
1c:
Each step I take I know that He will guide me;
To higher ground He ever leads me on.
Until some day the last step will be taken,
Each step I take just leads me closer home.
2:
2.
At times I feel my faith begin to waver,
When up ahead I see a chasm wide,
It's then I turn and look up to my Savior,
I am strong when He is by my side.
2c:
Each step I take I know that He will guide me;
To higher ground He ever leads me on.
Until some day the last step will be taken,
Each step I take just leads me closer home.
3:
3.
I trust in God, no matter come what may,
For life eternal is in His hand,
He holds the key that opens up the way,
That will lead me to the promised land.
3c:
Each step I take I know that He will guide me;
To higher ground He ever leads me on.
Until some day the last step will be taken,
Each step I take just leads me closer home.

129.title: Each Step I Take
129.credits:
Words and Music by: W. Elmo Mercer
Copyright © 1953 by Singspiration Music(ASCAP)(admin. By Brentwood-Benson Music Publishing, Inc.)
All Rights reserved. Used by permission.

1:
1.
Face to face with Christ my Savior,
Face to face what will it be,
When with rapture I behold Him,
Jesus Christ who died for me?
1c:
Face to face shall I behold Him,
Far beyond the starry sky;
Face to face, in all His glory,
I shall see Him by and by.
2:
2.
Only faintly now I see Him,
With the darkling veil between;
But a blessed day is coming,
When His glory shall be seen.
2c:
Face to face shall I behold Him,
Far beyond the starry sky;
Face to face, in all His glory,

3:
3.
What rejoicing in His presence,
When are banished grief and pain,

3c:
Face to face shall I behold Him,
Far beyond the starry sky;
Face to face, in all His glory,
I shall see Him by and by.
4:
4.
Face to face! O blissful moment!
Face to face - to see and know;
Face to face with my Redeemer,
Jesus Christ, who loves me so.
4c:
Face to face shall I behold Him,
Far beyond the starry sky;
Face to face, in all His glory,
I shall see Him by and by.

131.title: Face to Face

esp.title: Cara A Cara
esp.credits:
Palabras: Carrie E. Breck
Música: Grant Colfax Tullar

1:
1.
Encamped along the hills of light,
Ye Christian soldiers, rise,
And press the battle ere the night
Shall veil the glowing skies.  
Against the foe in vales below
Let all our strength be hurled;  
Faith is the victory, we know,
That overcomes the world.
1c:
Faith is the victory!  
Faith is the victory!  
O glorious victory
That overcomes the world.
2:
2.
His banner over us is love,
Our sword the Word of God;  
We tread the road the saints above
With shouts of triumph trod.  
By faith they, like a whirlwind's breath,
Swept on o'er every field;  
The faith by which they conquered death
Is still our shining shield.
2c:
Faith is the victory!  
Faith is the victory!  
O glorious victory
That overcomes the world.
3:
3.
On every hand the foe we find
Drawn up in dread array;
Let tents of ease be left behind,
And onward to the fray.
Salvation's helmet on each head,
With truth all girt about,
The earth shall tremble 'neath our tread,
And echo with our shout.
3c:
Faith is the victory!  
Faith is the victory!  
O glorious victory
That overcomes the world.
4c:
Faith is the victory!  
Faith is the victory!  
O glorious victory
That overcomes the world.

134.title: Faith is the Victory
134.credits:
Words by: John H. Yates
Music by: Ira D. Sankey

1:
1.
Faith of our fathers! living still,
In spite of dungeon, fire, and sword;
O how our hearts beat high with joy
Whenever we hear that glorious word:
Faith of our fathers, holy faith!
We will be true to Thee till death!
1c:
Faith of our fathers, holy faith!
We will be true to Thee till death!
2:
2.
Our fathers, chained in prisons dark,
Were still in heart and conscience free;
How sweet would be their children's fate,
If they, like them, could die for Thee!
3:
3.
Faith of our fathers! we will love
Both friend and foe in all our strife;
And preach Thee, too, as love knows how,
By kindly words and virtuous life:

Música: Henri F. Hemy

1:
1.
Fairest Lord Jesus! Ruler of all nature!
O Thou of God and man the Son!
Thee will I cherish, Thee will I honor,
Thou, my soul's glory, joy, and crown.
2:
2.
Fair are the meadows, Fairer still the woodlands,
Robed in the blooming garb of spring;
Jesus is fairer, Jesus is purer,
Who makes the woeful heart to sing.
3:
3.
Fair is the sunshine, Fairer still the moonlight,
And all the twinkling starry host:
Jesus shines brighter Jesus shines purer,
Than all the angels heaven can boast.
4:
4.
Beautiful Savior! Lord of the nations!
Son of God and Son of Man!
Glory and honor Praise, adoration,
Now and forever-more be Thine!

137.title: Fairest Lord Jesus

esp.title: Glorioso Cristo
esp.credits: Palabras: German Folk Hymn, Arr.: Muenster Gesangbuch, Tr.: Willis’s Church Chorals & Choir Studies, Tr., St. 4: Joseph A. SeissMúsica por: Fallersleben’s Schlesische Volkslieder, Arr.: Richard S. Willis

1:
1. 
Tempted and tried we're oft made to wonder 
Why it should be thus all the day long, 
While there are others living about us, 
Never molested though in the wrong.
1c:
Farther along we'll know all about it, 
Farther along we'll understand why; 
Cheer up, my brother live in the sunshine, 
We'll understand it all by and by.
2:
2.
When death has come and taken our loved ones,
It leaves our home so lonely and drear;
Then do we wonder why others prosper,
Living so wicked year after year.
2c:
Farther along we'll know all about it, 
Farther along we'll understand why; 
Cheer up, my brother live in the sunshine, 
We'll understand it all by and by.
3:
3. 
Faithful till death said our loving Master, 
A few more days to labor and wait; 
Toils of the road will then seem as nothing, 
As we sweep thru the beautiful gate.
3c:
Farther along we'll know all about it, 
Farther along we'll understand why; 
Cheer up, my brother live in the sunshine, 
We'll understand it all by and by.
4:
4. 
When we see Jesus coming in glory, 
When He comes from His home in the sky; 
Then we shall meet Him in that bright mansion, 
We'll understand it all by and by.
4c:
Farther along we'll know all about it, 
Farther along we'll understand why; 
Cheer up, my brother live in the sunshine, 
We'll understand it all by and by.

1:
1.
Father and Friend, Thy light, Thy love,

Thy glory gilds the heavens above,
And all the earth is full of Thee.
2:
2.
Thy voice we hear, Thy presence feel,
While Thou, too pure for mortal sight,
Enwrapt in clouds, invisible,
Reignest the Lord of life and light.
3:
3.
We know not in what hallowed part
Of the wide heavens Thy throne may be;
But this we know, that where Thou art,
Strength, wisdom, good-ness, dwell with Thee.
4:
4.
Thy children shall not faint nor fear,
Sustained by this delightful thought;

2:
2.
Father of mercies, God of love,
Whose gentle gifts all creatures share,

Proclaim to all Thy constant care,
Proclaim to all Thy constant care.
3:
3.
Father of mercies, may our hearts
Never overlook Thy bounteous care;

Still own in grateful praise and prayer,
Still own in grateful praise and prayer.

1.
For the beauty of the earth,
For the beauty of the skies,

Lord of all, to Thee we raise
This our sacrifice of praise.
2:
2.
For the beauty of each hour
Of the day and of the night,

2c:
Lord of all, to Thee we raise
This our sacrifice of praise.
3:
3.
For the joy of human love,
Brother, sister, parent, child,

3c:
Lord of all, to Thee we raise
This our sacrifice of praise.
4:
4.
For Thy church that evermore

4c:
Lord of all, to Thee we raise
This our sacrifice of praise.

Down at the cross where my Savior died,

Glory to His name.
2:
2.
I am so wondrously saved from sin,
Jesus so sweetly abides within,

Glory to His name.

1:
1.
"Give me thy heart," says the Father above,
No gift so precious to Him as our love,
Softly He whispers wherever thou art,
"Gratefully trust me, and give me thy heart."
1c:
"Give me thy heart, Give me thy heart,"
Hear the soft whisper, wherever thou art;
From this dark world, He would draw thee apart,
Speaking so tenderly, "Give me thy heart."
2:
2.
"Give me thy heart," says the Savior of men,
Calling in mercy again and again;
"Turn now from sin, and from evil depart.
Have I not died for thee? Give me thy heart."
2c:
"Give me thy heart, Give me thy heart,"
Hear the soft whisper, wherever thou art;
From this dark world, He would draw thee apart,
Speaking so tenderly, "Give me thy heart."
3:
3.
"Give me thy heart," says the Spirit divine,

3c:
"Give me thy heart, Give me thy heart,"
Hear the soft whisper, wherever thou art;
From this dark world, He would draw thee apart,
Speaking so tenderly, "Give me thy heart."

3.
Calvary's mournful mountain climb;

Learn of Jesus Christ to die.
4:
4.
Early hasten to the tomb

3.

God Himself is with us

2:
2.
God Himself is with us;

Serve with awe in heavenly regions.

To us here;
Hear, O Christ, the praises

3:
3.
O Thou fount of blessing,

Trusting only in Thy merit,

Who behold Thy glory,

2c:
God is love!  God is love!  
Come, let us all unite to sing That God is love.
3c:
God is love!  God is love!  
Come, let us all unite to sing That God is love.

1.
God is the fountain whence

2.
The comforts He affords

He is the source of fresh delights,
My portion and my all.
3:
3.
He fills my heart with joy,
My lips attunes for praise;

The remnant of my days.

1:
1.
God of grace and God of glory,
On Thy people pour Thy power;

Grant us wisdom, Grant us courage,
For the facing of this hour,
For the facing of this hour.
2:
2.
Lo! The hosts of evil round us
Scorn Thy Christ, assail His ways!
Fears and doubts too long have bound us,
Free our hearts to work and praise.
Grant us wisdom, Grant us courage,
For the living of these days,
For the living of these days.
3:
3.
Cure Thy children's warring madness,
Bend our pride to Thy control;
Shame our wanton, selfish gladness

Grant us wisdom, Grant us courage,
Lest we miss Thy kingdom's goal,
Lest we miss Thy kingdom's goal.
4:
4.
Set our feet on lofty places;

In the fight to set men free.
Grant us wisdom, Grant us courage,
That we fail not man nor Thee!
That we fail not man nor Thee!
5:
5.
Save us from weak resignation
To the evils we deplore;
Let the search for Thy salvation 
Be our glory evermore.
Grant us wisdom, Grant us courage,
Serving Thee whom we adore,
Serving Thee whom we adore.

187.title: God of Grace and God of Glory

Words: Harry E. Fosdick

1:
1.
Marvelous grace of our loving Lord,

1c:
Grace, grace, God's grace,
Grace that will pardon and cleanse within;  
Grace, grace, God's grace,
Grace that is greater than all our sin.
2:
2.
Sin and despair like the sea waves cold,
Threaten the soul with infinite loss;
Grace that is greater, yes, grace untold,
Point to the refuge, the mighty cross.
2c:
Grace, grace, God's grace,
Grace that will pardon and cleanse within;  
Grace, grace, God's grace,
Grace that is greater than all our sin.
3:
3.
Dark is the stain that we cannot hide,
What can avail to wash it away?  
Look!  there is flowing a crimson tide;  
Whiter than snow you may be to-day.
3c:
Grace, grace, God's grace,
Grace that will pardon and cleanse within;  
Grace, grace, God's grace,
Grace that is greater than all our sin.
4:
4.
Marvelous, infinite, matchless grace,
Freely bestowed on all who believe;

Will you this moment His grace receive?
4c:
Grace, grace, God's grace,
Grace that will pardon and cleanse within;  
Grace, grace, God's grace,
Grace that is greater than all our sin.

2.

and springtime and harvest,

3:
3.
Pardon for sin and a peace that endureth,

1:
1.
Be not dismayed whate'er betide,

God will take care of you,
Thro' every day,

He will take care of you,
God will take care of you.
2:
2.

God will take care of you,
Thro' every day,

3.

God will take care of you,
Thro' every day,

He will take care of you,

4:

No matter what may be the test,

4c:
God will take care of you,
Thro' every day,
O'er all the way;
He will take care of you,
God will take care of you.

2.

1:
1.
Guide me, O Thou great Jehovah,
Pilgrim through this barren land;
I am weak, but Thou art mighty,
Hold me with Thy powerful hand;
Bread of heaven,

2.
Open now the crystal fountain,

Let the fiery, cloudy pillar,

Strong Deliverer,

1:
1.
Have Thine own way, Lord!
Have Thine own way!

2:
2.
Have Thine own way, Lord!

3:
3.

4:
4.

Let them praises give Jehovah,

2.

Let them praises give Jehovah,

3.
All ye fruitful trees and cedars,

3c:
Let them praises give Jehovah,
