Music: Tullius C. O’Kane

esp.title: Estando A Orillas Del Jordan
esp.credits:
Palabras: Samuel Stennett / Cho.: Tullius C. O’Kane
Musica: Tullius C. O’Kane

1:
1.
Oft we come together,
Oft we sing and pray;
Here we bring our offering
On this holy day.
1c:
Help us Lord,
Thy love to see,
May we all in truth and spirit Worship Thee.
2:
2.
May we keep in memory,
All that Thou hast said,
May we truly worship
As we eat the bread.
2c:
Help us Lord,
Thy love to see,
May we all in truth and spirit Worship Thee.
3:
3.
May we all in spirit,
All with one ac-cord,
Take this cup of blessing,
Given by the Lord.
3c:
Help us Lord,
Thy love to see,
May we all in truth and spirit Worship Thee.

511.title: Oft We Come Together
511.credits: Words and Music: Tillit S. Teddlie

esp.title: Cuando Nos Juntamos A Cantar
esp.credits: Palabras y Musica por: Tillit S. Teddlie

1:
1.
On the cross of Calvary,
Jesus died for thee and me;
There He shed His precious blood,
That from sin we might be free.
O the cleansing stream doth flow,
And it washes white as snow:
It was for me that Jesus died
On the cross of Calvary.
1c:
On Calvary, on Calvary.
It was for me that Jesus died
On the cross of Calvary.
2:
2.
O that wondrous, wondrous love,
Brought me down at Jesus' feet!
O such wondrous, dying love,
Asks a sacrifice complete!
Lord, I give myself to Thee,
Soul and body Thine to be:
It was for me Thy blood was shed
On the cross of Calvary.
2c:
On Calvary, on Calvary.
It was for me that Jesus died
On the cross of Calvary.
3:
3.
Take me, Jesus, I am Thine,
Wholly Thine for evermore;
Blessed Jesus, Thou art mine,
Dwell within for evermore.
Cleanse, O cleanse my heart for sin,
Make and keep me pure within:
It was for this Thy blood was shed
On the cross of Calvary.
3c:
On Calvary, on Calvary.
It was for me that Jesus died
On the cross of Calvary.
4:
4.
Clouds and darkness veiled the sky,
When the Lord was crucified;
"It is finished!" was His cry,
When He bowed His head and died.
It was finished there for me;
All the world may now go free:
It was for me that Jesus died
On the cross of Calvary.
4c:
On Calvary, on Calvary.
It was for me that Jesus died
On the cross of Calvary.

514.title: On the Cross of Calvary
514.credits:
Words: Gill’s & McLaughlin’s Good News in Song / Lizzie DeArmond
Music: Gill’s & McLaughlin’s Good News in Song / Arr.: William J. Kirkpatrick

1:
1.
On Zion's glorious summit stood
A numerous host redeemed by blood!  
They hymned their King in strains divine;  
I heard the song and strove to join,
I heard the song and strove to join.
2:
2.
Here all who suffered sword or flame
For truth, or Jesus' lovely name,
Shout victory now and hail the Lamb,
And bow before the great I AM,
And bow before the great I AM.
3:
3.
While everlasting ages roll,
Eternal love shall feast their soul,
And scenes of bliss, for ever new,
Rise in succession to their view,
Rise in succession to their view.
sanctus:
Holy, holy, holy Lord,
God of hosts, on high adored!  
Who like me Thy praise should sing,
O Almighty King!  
Holy, holy, holy Lord,
God of hosts, on high adored!  
Holy, holy, holy.

515.title: On Zion’s Glorious Summit
515.credits:
Words: John Kent
Music: Robert Skene

esp.title: Sobre Sión
esp.credits:
Palabras: John Kent
Música: Robert Skene

1:
1.
Only in Thee, O Savior mine,
Dwelleth my soul in peace divine,
Peace that the world, though all combine,
Never can take from me.  
Pleasures of earth, so seemingly sweet,
Fail at the last my longings to meet;  
Only in Thee my bliss is complete,
Only, dear Lord, in Thee!
2:
2.
Only in Thee a radiance bright,
Shines like a beacon in the night,
Guiding my pilgrim bark aright,
Over life's trackless sea.  
Only in Thee, when troubles molest,
When with temptation I am oppressed,
There is a sweet pavilion of rest,
Only, dear Lord, in Thee!
3:
3.
Only in Thee, when days are drear,
When neither sun nor stars appear,
Still I can trust and feel no fear,
Sing when I cannot see.  
Only in Thee, whatever betide,
All of my need is freely supplied;  
There is no hope nor helper beside,
Only, dear Lord, in Thee!
4:
4.
Only in Thee, dear Savior, slain,
Losing Thy life my own to gain,
Trusting, I'm cleansed from every stain;
Thou art my only plea.  
Only in Thee my heart will delight,
Till in that land where cometh no night
Faith will be lost in heavenly sight,
Only, dear Lord, in Thee!

519.title: Only in Thee
519.credits:
Words: T. O. Chisholm
Music: Charles H. Gabriel

esp.title: Solo En Ti
esp.credits:
Palabras: T. O. Chisholm
Musica: Charles H. Gabriel

1:
1.
Hear the sweet voice of Jesus say,
"Come unto me, I am the way;"
Harken, the loving call obey;
Come for He loves you so.
1c:
Only a step, only a step:
Come, for He bled for you and died;
He's the same loving Savior yet,
Jesus the Crucified.
2:
2.
Casting your heavy burden down,
Come to the cross, the world may frown;
Yet you shall wear a glorious crown,
When He makes up His own.
2c:
Only a step, only a step:
Come, for He bled for you and died;
He's the same loving Savior yet,
Jesus the Crucified.
3c:
Only a step, only a step:
Come, for He bled for you and died;
He's the same loving Savior yet,
Jesus the Crucified.

esp.title: Solo Un Paso
esp.credits: Palabras Y Musica: Charles H. Gabriel

1:
1.
There is, beyond the azure blue,
A God, concealed from human sight.
He tinted skies with heavenly hue
And framed the worlds with His great might.
1c:
There is a God, He is alive,
In Him we live, and we survive;
From dust our God created man,
He is our God, the great I AM.
2:
2.
There was, a long, long time ago.
A God whose voice the prophets heard.
He is the God that we should know,
Who speaks from His inspired Word.
2c:
There is a God, He is alive,
In Him we live, and we survive;
From dust our God created man,
He is our God, the great I AM.
3:
3.
Secure, is life from mortal mind,
God holds the germ within His hand,
Though men may search, they cannot find,
For God alone does understand.
3c:
There is a God, He is alive,
In Him we live, and we survive;
From dust our God created man,
He is our God, the great I AM.
4:
4.
Our God, whose Son upon a tree,
A life was willing there to give,
That He from sin might set man free,
And evermore with Him could live.
4c:
There is a God, He is alive,
In Him we live, and we survive;
From dust our God created man,
He is our God, the great I AM.
End

esp.title: Nuestro Dios Vive Hoy

528.title: Praise God From Whom All Blessings Flow

Words by: Thomas Ken
Music by: Louis Bourgeois

esp.title: A Dios Quien Bendice, Load
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Thomas Ken
Musica por: Louis Bourgeois

1:
1.
Out of my bondage, sorrow and night,
Jesus, I come, Jesus, I come;
Into Thy freedom, gladness and light,
Jesus, I come to Thee;
Out of my sickness, into Thy health,
Out of my want and into Thy wealth,
Out of my sin and into Thyself,
Jesus, I come to Thee.
2:
2.
Out of my shameful failure and loss,
Jesus, I come, Jesus, I come;
Into the glorious gain of Thy cross,
Jesus, I come to Thee;
Out of earth's sorrows, into Thy balm,
Out of life's storms and into Thy calm,
Out of distress to jubilant psalm,
Jesus, I come to Thee.
3:
3.
Out of unrest and arrogant pride,
Jesus, I come, Jesus, I come;
Into Thy blessed will to abide,
Jesus, I come to Thee;
Out of myself to dwell in Thy love,
Out of despair into raptures above,
Upward for aye on wings of a dove,
Jesus, I come to Thee.
4:
4.
Out of the fear and dread of the tomb,
Jesus, I come, Jesus, I come;
Into the joy and light of Thy home,
Jesus, I come to Thee;
Out of the depths of ruin untold,
Into the peace of Thy sheltering fold,
Ever Thy glorious face to behold,
Jesus, I come to Thee.

529.title: Out of My Bondage
529.credits:

Music: George C. Stebbins

esp.title: Vengo, Jesús A Ti

1c:
Hallelujah!  
Amen, Amen,
Amen, Amen.
2c:
Hallelujah!  
Amen, Amen,
Amen, Amen.
3c:
Hallelujah!  
Amen, Amen,
Amen, Amen.
4c:
Hallelujah!  
Amen, Amen,
Amen, Amen.

531.title: Praise the Lord
531.credits:
Words by: J. Kempthorne
Music by: Lowell Mason

1c:
Praise Him!  praise Him!  tell of His excellent greatness;
Praise Him!  praise Him! ever in joyful song!
2:
2.
Praise Him!  praise Him!  Jesus, our blessed Redeemer!  
For our sins He suffered, and bled, and died;  
He, our Rock, our hope of eternal salvation;  
Hail Him!  hail Him!  Jesus the Crucified!  
Sound His praises!  Jesus, who bore our sorrows,
Love unbounded wonderful, deep and strong:
2c:
Praise Him!  praise Him!  tell of His excellent greatness;
Praise Him!  praise Him! ever in joyful song!
3:
3.
Praise Him!  praise Him!  Jesus, our blessed Redeemer!  
Heavenly portals loud with hosannas ring!  
Jesus, Savior, reigneth for ever and ever;  
Crown Him!  crown Him!  Prophet, and Priest, and King!  
Christ is coming!  o-ver the world victorious,
Power and glory unto the Lord be-long:
3c:
Praise Him!  praise Him!  tell of His excellent greatness;
Praise Him!  praise Him! ever in joyful song!

532.title: Praise Him! Praise Him!
532.credits:
Words by: Fanny J. Crosby
Music by: Chester G. Allen

esp.title: ¡Alabadle!
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Fanny J. Crosby
Musica por: Chester G. Allen

1:
1.
Praise to the Lord, the Almighty, the King of creation!
O my soul, praise Him for He is thy health and salvation!
All ye who hear, Now to His temple draw near;
Join me in glad adoration!
2:
2.
Praise to the Lord, who o'er all things so wondrously reigneth,
Shieldeth thee under His wings, yea so gently sustaineth!
Hast thou not seen How thy desires e'er have been
Granted in what He ordaineth?
3:
3.
Praise to the Lord, who doth prosper thy work and defend thee;
Surely His goodness and mercy here daily attend thee.
Ponder anew What the Almighty can do,
If with His love He befriend thee.
4:
4.
Praise to the Lord! O let all that is in me adore Him!
All that hath life and breath, come now with praises before Him!
Let the amen Sound from His people again;
Gladly forever adore Him.

534.title: Praise to the Lord, The Almighty
534.credits:
Words: Joachin Neander / tr. Catherine Winkworth
Music: Stralsund Gesangbuch / har. W. Sterndale Bennett

1:
1. 
Careless soul, why will you linger,
Wandering from the fold of God?
Hear you not the invitation?
O prepare to meet thy God.
1c:
Careless soul, O heed the warning,
For you life will soon be gone;
O how sad to face the judgment,
Unprepared to meet thy God.
2:
2. 
Why so thoughtless are you standing 
While the fleeting years go by,
And your life is spent in folly? 
O prepare to meet thy God.
2c:
Careless soul, O heed the warning,
For you life will soon be gone;
O how sad to face the judgment,
Unprepared to meet thy God.
3:
3. 
Hear you not the earnest pleadings
 Of your friends who wish thee well?
And perhaps before tomorrow
You'll be called to meet thy God.
3c:
Careless soul, O heed the warning,
For you life will soon be gone;
O how sad to face the judgment,
Unprepared to meet thy God.
4:
4. 
If you spurn the invitation
Till the Spirit shall depart,
Then you'll see your sad condition,
Unprepared to meet thy God.
4c:
Careless soul, O heed the warning,
For you life will soon be gone;
O how sad to face the judgment,
Unprepared to meet thy God.

esp.title: Prepárate A Ver Tu Dios
esp.credits: Palabras y Musica: James Henry Stanley

2:
2.
For the blessings of the field,
For the stores the gardens yield,
For the joy which harvests bring,
Grateful praises now we sing.
3:
3.
As Thy prospering hand hath blest,
May we give Thee of our best;
And by deeds of kindly love
For Thy mercies grateful prove.

539.title: Praise to God, Immortal Praise
539.credits:
Words: Anna L. Barbauld
Music: Ignaz J. Pleyel

1:
1.
Prince of Peace! control my will,
Bid this struggling heart be still;
Bid my fears and doubtings cease,
Hush my spirit into peace.
2:
2.
Thou hast bought me with Thy blood,
Opened wide the gate of God;
Peace I ask, but peace must be,
Lord, in being one with Thee.

Palabras: Mary A. S. Barber
Música: W. T. Porter

542.title: Purer in Heart, O God
542.credits:
Words by: Fannie E. C. Davison
Music by: James H. Fillmore

1c:
I'm redeemed by love divine,
Glory, glory, Christ is mine, Christ is mine,
All to Him I now resign, 
I have been redeemed!
2c:
I'm redeemed by love divine,
Glory, glory, Christ is mine, Christ is mine,
All to Him I now resign, 
I have been redeemed!
3c:
I'm redeemed by love divine,
Glory, glory, Christ is mine, Christ is mine,
All to Him I now resign, 
I have been redeemed!

543.title: Redeemed
543.credits:
Words: James Rowe
Music: Stancel A. Ganus

1:
1.
Redeemed how I love to proclaim it!  
Redeemed by the blood of the Lamb;  
Redeemed thru His infinite mercy,
His child, and forever, I am.
1c:
Redeemed, redeemed,
Redeemed by the blood of the Lamb;  
Redeemed, redeemed,
His child, and forever, I am.
2:
2.
Redeemed and so happy in Jesus,
No language my rapture can tell;  
I know that the light of His presence
With me doth continually dwell.
2c:
Redeemed, redeemed,
Redeemed by the blood of the Lamb;  
Redeemed, redeemed,
His child, and forever, I am.
3:
3.
I know I shall see in His beauty
The King in whose law I delight;  
Who lovingly guardeth my footsteps,
And giveth me songs in the night.
3c:
Redeemed, redeemed,
Redeemed by the blood of the Lamb;  
Redeemed, redeemed,
His child, and forever, I am.
End

544.title: Redeemed
544.credits:
Words by: Fanny J. Crosby
Music by: William J. Kirkpatrick

esp.title: Comprado Con Sangre
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Fanny J. Crosby
Música por: William J. Kirkpatrick
esp.translation_copyright: J. R. Rios y W. C. Brand

1:
1.
Take my life, O Father, mold it
In obedience to Thy will;
And as ripening years unfold it,
Keep it true and childlike still.
2:
2.
Father, keep it pure and lowly,
Strong and brave, yet free from strife,
Turning from the paths unholy
Of a vain or sinful life.
3:
3.
Ever let Thy might surround it;
Strengthen it with power divine
Till Thy cords of love have bound it,
Father, wholly unto Thine.

545.title: Take My Life, O Father, Mold It
545.credits:
Words: Bartol’s Hymns for the Sanctuary / Arr.: Hymn Tune & Service Book
Music: John A. Stevenson

1:
1.
Rejoice, the Lord is King:
Your Lord and King adore!
Rejoice, give thanks, and sing,
And triumph evermore:
2:
2.
Jesus, the Savior, reigns,
The God of truth and love;
When He had purged our stains,
He took His seat above:
3:
3.
His Kingdom cannot fail,
He rules over earth and heaven;
The keys of death and hell
Are to our Jesus given:
4:
4.
Rejoice, in glorious hope!
Our Lord and Judge shall come,
And take His servants up
To their eternal home.

547.title: Rejoice, the Lord Is King
547.credits:
Words: Charles Wesley
Music: John Darwall / Arr.: Aaron Williams

1:
1.
Rise up, O men of God!
Have done with lesser things;
Give heart and mind and soul and strength
To serve the King of kings.
2:
2.
Rise up, O men of God!
His kingdom tarries long;
Bring in the day of brotherhood
And end the night of wrong.
3:
3.
Rise up, O men of God!
The Church for you doth wait,
Her strength unequal to her task.
Rise up, and make her great!
4:
4.
Lift high the cross of Christ!
Tread where His feet have trod.
As brothers of the Son of Man,
Rise up, O men of God!

554.title: Rise Up, O Men of God
554.credits:
Words by: William P. Merrill
Music by: Aaron Williams

1:
1.
Rock of Ages, cleft for me,
Let me hide myself in Thee;
Let the water and the blood,
From Thy riven side which flowed,
Be of sin the double cure,
Cleanse me from its guilt and power.
2:
2.
Not the labor of my hands
Can fulfill the law's demands;
Could my zeal no respite know,
Could my tears for ever flow,
All for sin could not atone,
Thou must save and Thou alone.

557.title: Rock of Ages
557.credits:
Words by: A. M. Toplady
Music by: Thomas Hastings

esp.title: Roca De La Eternidad
esp.credits:
Palabras por: A. M. Toplady
Musica por: Thomas Hastings

1:
1.
Savior, like a shepherd lead us:
Much we need Thy tenderest care;  
In Thy pleasant pastures feed us,
For our use Thy folds prepare:
Blessed Jesus, Blessed Jesus,
Thou hast bought us, Thine we are;  
Blessed Jesus, Blessed Jesus,
Thou hast bought us, Thine we are.
3:
3.
Thou hast promised to receive us,
Poor and sinful though we be;  
Thou hast mercy to relieve us,
Grace to cleanse, and power to free:
Blessed Jesus, Blessed Jesus,
Early let us turn to Thee;  
Blessed Jesus, Blessed Jesus,
Early let us turn to Thee.
4:
4.
Early let us seek Thy favor,
Early let us do Thy will;  
Blessed Lord and only Savior,
With Thy love our bosoms fill:
Blessed Jesus, Blessed Jesus,
Thou hast loved us, love us still;  
Blessed Jesus, Blessed Jesus,
Thou hast loved us, love us still.

564.title: Savior, Like a Shepherd Lead Us
564.credits:
Words: Thrupp’s Hymns For The Young
Music: William B. Bradbury

esp.title: Cristo Cual Pastor Nos Guía
esp.credits:
Palabras: Thrupp’s Hymns For The Young
Musica: William B. Bradbury

1:
1.
Savior, Thy dying love
Thou gavest me;
Nor should I aught withhold,
Dear Lord, from Thee:
In love my soul would bow,
My heart fulfill its vow,
Some offering bring Thee now,
Something for Thee.
2:
2.
At the blest mercy seat, 
Pleading for me,
My feeble faith looks up,
Jesus, to Thee:
Help me the cross to bear,
Thy wondrous love declare,
Some song to raise, or prayer,
Something for Thee.
3:
3.
Give me a faithful heart—
Likeness to Thee—
That each departing day,
Henceforth may see
Some work of love begun,
Some deed of kindness done,
Some wanderer sought and won,
Something for Thee.
4:
4.
All that I am and have—
Thy gifts so free—
In joy, in grief, through life,
Dear Lord, for Thee!
And when Thy face I see,
My ransomed soul shall be,
Through all eternity,
Something for Thee.

565.title: Savior, Thy Dying Love
565.credits:
Words: Sylvanus D. Phelps
Music: Robert Lowry

esp.title: Cristo, Tu Santo Amor
esp.credits:
Palabras: Sylvanus D. Phelps
Música: Robert Lowry

1:
1. 
Savior, teach me, day by day,
Love's sweet lesson to obey:
Sweeter lesson cannot be–
Loving Him who first loved me.
2:
2. 
With a childlike heart of love,
At Thy bidding may I move,
Prompt to serve and follow Thee–
Loving Him who first loved me.
3:
3. 
Teach me all Thy steps to trace,
Strong to follow in Thy grace,
Learning how to love from Thee–
Loving Him who first loved me.
4:
4. 
Love in loving finds employ,
In obedience all her joy;
Ever new that joy will be–
Loving Him who first loved me.

568.title: Savior, Teach Me
568.credits:
Words: Jane E. Leeson
Music: Carl Maria von Weber / arr.: Henry W. Greatorex

569.title: Search Me, O God
569.credits:
Words by: J. Edwin Orr
Music by: Maori Folk Melody, Arrangement by: Otis L. McCoy

1c:
Sing on, O blissful music!
With every note you raise
My heart is filled with rapture,
My soul is lost in praise;
Sing on, O blissful music!
With every note you raise
My heart is filled with rapture,
My soul is lost in praise.
2:
2.
Sing on, ye joyful pilgrims,
While here on earth we stay;
Let songs of home and Jesus
Beguile each fleeting day;
Sing on the grand old story
Of His redeeming love,
The everlasting chorus
That fills the realms above.
2c:
Sing on, O blissful music!
With every note you raise
My heart is filled with rapture,
My soul is lost in praise;
Sing on, O blissful music!
With every note you raise
My heart is filled with rapture,
My soul is lost in praise.
3:
3.
Sing on, ye joyful pilgrims:
The time will not be long,
Till in our Father's kingdom
We swell a nobler song,
Where those we love are waiting
To greet us on the shore;
We'll meet beyond the river,
Where surges roll no more.
3c:
Sing on, O blissful music!
With every note you raise
My heart is filled with rapture,
My soul is lost in praise;
Sing on, O blissful music!
With every note you raise
My heart is filled with rapture,
My soul is lost in praise.

581.title: Sing On
581.credits:
Words: Fanny J. Crosby
Music: John R. Sweney

esp.title: Cantad Oh Peregrinos
esp.credits:
Palabras: Fanny J. Crosby
Musica: John R. Sweney

1:
1.
Sing to me of heaven, sing that song of peace,
From the toils that bind me it will bring release;
Burdens will be lifted that are pressing so,
Showers of great blessing o'er my heart will flow.
1c:
Sing to me of heaven, let me fondly dream
Of its golden glory, Of its pearly gleam;
Sing to me when shadows of the evening fall,
Sing to me of heaven, Sweetest song of all.
2:
2.
Sing to me of heaven, as I walk alone,
Dreaming of the comrades that so long have gone;
In a fairer region among the angel throng,
They are happy as they sing that old, sweet song.
2c:
Sing to me of heaven, let me fondly dream
Of its golden glory, Of its pearly gleam;
Sing to me when shadows of the evening fall,
Sing to me of heaven, Sweetest song of all.
3:
3.
Sing to me of heaven, tenderly and low,
Till the shadows o'er me rise and swiftly go;
When my heart is weary, when the day is long,
Sing to me of heaven, sing that old, sweet song.
3c:
Sing to me of heaven, let me fondly dream
Of its golden glory, Of its pearly gleam;
Sing to me when shadows of the evening fall,
Sing to me of heaven, Sweetest song of all.

583.title: Sing to Me of Heaven
583.credits:
Words by: Ada Powell
Music by: Benjamin Burk Beall

esp.title: Cantame Del Cielo
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Ada Powell
Musica por: Benjamin Burk Beall

1:
1.
Soldiers of Christ, arise
And put your armor on;  
Strong in the strength which God supplies,
Strong in the strength which God supplies,
Through His beloved Son.
2:
2.
Strong in the Lord of hosts
And in His mighty power;  
Who in the strength of Jesus trusts,
Who in the strength of Jesus trusts,
Is more than conqueror.
3:
3.
Stand, then, in His great might,
With all His strength endued;  
But take, to arm you for the fight,
But take, to arm you for the fight,
The panoply of God.
4:
4.
Leave no unguarded place,
No weakness of the soul;  
Take every virtue, every grace,
Take every virtue, every grace,
And fortify the whole.
5:
5.
That having all things done,
And all your conflicts past,
You may overcome through Christ alone,
You may overcome through Christ alone,
And stand entire at last.

585.title: Soldiers of Christ, Arise
585.credits:
Words by: Charles Wesley
Music by: Bradbury’s Jubilee

1:
1. 
Soul, a Savior thou are needing!
Soul a Savior waits for thee!
Hear His words of tender pleading,
Hear His gracious "Come to Me."
1c:
He is calling, softly calling,
On thine ear His voice is falling;
He is calling, softly calling,
"Come to Me and be at rest."
2:
2. 
He has died for thy transgression,
If thou wilt, thou canst be free;
Soul, He waits for thy confession,"
"Savior, I will go to Thee."
2c:
He is calling, softly calling,
On thine ear His voice is falling;
He is calling, softly calling,
"Come to Me and be at rest."
3:
3. 
Do not linger till the morrow,
Let thy loving answer be,
"Savior, in my joy or sorrow, 
I will ever go to Thee."
3c:
He is calling, softly calling,
On thine ear His voice is falling;
He is calling, softly calling,
"Come to Me and be at rest."

587.title: Soul, a Savior Thou Art Needing
587.credits:
Words: Jessie Brown Pounds
Music: James H. Fillmore

esp.title: Necesitas Un Salvador
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Jessie Brown Pounds
Musica por: James H. Fillmore

1:
1.
Sinners Jesus will receive:
Sound this word of grace to all
Who the heavenly pathway leave,
All who linger, all who fall.
1c:
Sing it o'er and o'er again:
Christ receiveth sinful men;
Make the message clear and plain:
Christ receiveth sinful men.
2:
2.
Come, and He will give you rest:
Trust Him, for His word is plain;
He will take the sinfulest:
Christ receiveth sinful men.
2c:
Sing it o'er and o'er again:
Christ receiveth sinful men;
Make the message clear and plain:
Christ receiveth sinful men.
3:
3.
Now my heart condemns me not:
Pure before the law I stand;
He who cleansed me from all spot
Satisfied its last demand.
3c:
Sing it o'er and o'er again:
Christ receiveth sinful men;
Make the message clear and plain:
Christ receiveth sinful men.
4:
4.
Christ receiveth sinful men,
Even me with all my sin;
Purged from every spot and stain,
Heav'n with Him I enter in.
4c:
Sing it o'er and o'er again:
Christ receiveth sinful men;
Make the message clear and plain:
Christ receiveth sinful men.

588.title: Sinners Jesus Will Receive
588.credits:
Words: Erdmann Neumeister / tr. Emma F. Bevan / Ref: James McGranahan
Music: James McGranahan

esp.title: Cristo Acoge Al Pecador
esp.credits:
Palabras: Erdmann Neumeister / tr. Emma F. Bevan / Ref: James McGranahan
Música: James McGranahan

1:
1.
Are you sowing the seed of the kingdom, brother,
In the morning bright and fair?
Are you sowing the seed of the kingdom, brother,
In the heat of the noonday's glare?
1c:
For the harvest-time is coming on,
And the reapers' work will soon be done;
Will your sheaves be many?
Will you garner any,
For the gathering at the harvest home?
2:
2.
Are you sowing the seed of the kingdom, brother,
In the still and solemn night?
Are you sowing the seed of the kingdom, brother,
For a harvest pure and white?
2c:
For the harvest-time is coming on,
And the reapers' work will soon be done;
Will your sheaves be many?
Will you garner any,
For the gathering at the harvest home?
3:
3.
Are you sowing the seed of the kingdom, brother,
All along the fertile way?
Are you sowing the seed of the kingdom, brother?
You must reap at the last great day!
3c:
For the harvest-time is coming on,
And the reapers' work will soon be done;
Will your sheaves be many?
Will you garner any,
For the gathering at the harvest home?

589.title: Sowing the Seed of the Kingdom

1:
1.
Sun of my soul, Thou Savior dear,
It is not night if Thou be near;
O may no earthborn cloud arise
To hide Thee from Thy servant's eyes.
2:
2.
When the soft dews of kindly sleep
My wearied eyelids gently steep,
Be my last thought, how sweet to rest
For ever on my Savior's breast.
3:
3.
Abide with me from morn till eve,
For without Thee I cannot live;
Abide with me when night is nigh,
For without Thee I dare not die.
4:
4.
Come near and bless us when we wake,
Ere through the world our way we take;
Till, in the ocean of Thy love,
We lose ourselves  in heaven above.

593.title: Sun of My Soul
593.credits:
Words: John Keble
Music: Katholisches Gesangbuch / Arr.: William H. Monk

esp.title: Sol De Mi Alma
esp.credits:
Palabras por: John Keble
Musica por: Katholisches Gesangbuch / Arr.: William H. Monk

1:
1.
Stand up, stand up for Jesus!  
Ye soldiers of the cross;  
Lift high His royal banner,
It must not suffer loss;  
From victory unto victory 
His army shall He lead,
Till every foe is vanquished,
And Christ is Lord in-deed.
3:
3.
Stand up, stand up for Jesus!  
Stand in His strength alone;  
The arm of flesh will fail you,
Ye dare not trust your own;  
Put on the gospel armor,
And watching unto prayer,
Where duty calls, or danger,
Be never wanting there.

595.title: Stand Up, Stand Up for Jesus
595.credits:
Words by: George Duffield
Music by: George J. Webb

esp.title: Paraos En Pie Por Cristo
esp.credits:
Palabras por: George Duffield
Musica por: George J. Webb

1:
1.
Standing on the promises of Christ my King, 
Thru eternal ages let His praises ring; 
Glory in the highest I will shout and sing,
Standing on the promises of God.
1c:
Standing, standing,
Standing on the promises of God my Savior, 
Standing, standing, 
I'm standing on the promises of God.
2:
2.
Standing on the promises that can-not fail, 
When the howling storms of doubt and fear assail, 
By the living word of God I shall prevail,
Standing on the promises of God.
2c:
Standing, standing,
Standing on the promises of God my Savior, 
Standing, standing, 
I'm standing on the promises of God.
3:
3.
Standing on the promises of Christ the Lord, 
Bound to Him eternally by love's strong cord, 
Overcoming daily with the Spirit's sword,
Standing on the promises of God.
3c:
Standing, standing,
Standing on the promises of God my Savior, 
Standing, standing, 
I'm standing on the promises of God.
4:
4.
Standing on the promises I can-not fall, 
Listening every moment to the Spirit's call, 
Resting in my Savior as my all in all,
Standing on the promises of God.
4c:
Standing, standing,
Standing on the promises of God my Savior, 
Standing, standing, 
I'm standing on the promises of God.

598.title: Standing on the Promises
598.credits: Words and Music by: R. Kelso Carter

esp.title: Parado En Las Promesas
esp.credits: Palabras y Musica por: R. Kelso Carter

1:
1.
Trying to walk in the steps of the Savior,
Trying to follow our Savior and King;
Shaping our lives by His blessed example,
Happy, how happy, the songs that we bring.
1c:
How beautiful to walk in the steps of the Savior,
Stepping in the light,
Stepping in the light,
How beautiful to walk in the steps of the Savior,
Led in paths of light!
2:
2.
Pressing more closely to Him who is leading,
When we are tempted to turn from the way;
Trusting the arm that is strong to defend us,
Happy, how happy, our praises each day.
2c:
How beautiful to walk in the steps of the Savior,
Stepping in the light,
Stepping in the light,
How beautiful to walk in the steps of the Savior,
Led in paths of light!
3:
3.
Walking in footsteps of gentle forbearance,
Footsteps of faithfulness, mercy and love;
Looking to Him for the grace freely promised,
Happy, how happy, our journey above!
3c:
How beautiful to walk in the steps of the Savior,
Stepping in the light,
Stepping in the light,
How beautiful to walk in the steps of the Savior,
Led in paths of light!
4:
4.
Trying to walk in the steps of the Savior,
Upward, still upward we'll follow our Guide;
When we shall see Him, "the King in His beauty,"
Happy, how happy, our place at His side!
4c:
How beautiful to walk in the steps of the Savior,
Stepping in the light,
Stepping in the light,
How beautiful to walk in the steps of the Savior,
Led in paths of light!

599.title: Stepping in the Light
599.credits:
Words: Eliza E. Hewitt
Music: William J. Kirkpatrick

esp.title: Las Pisadas Del Maestro
esp.credits:
Palabras: Eliza E. Hewitt
Musica: William J. Kirkpatrick

1:
1.
Take my life, and let it be
Consecrated, Lord, to Thee;
Take my moments and my days,
Let them flow in ceaseless praise,
Let them flow in ceaseless praise.
2:
2.
Take my hands and let them move
At the impulse of Thy love;
Take my feet, and let them be
Swift and beautiful for Thee,
Swift and beautiful for Thee.
3:
3.
Take my voice, and let me sing
Always, only, for my King;
Take my lips, and let them be
Filled with messages from Thee,
Filled with messages from Thee.
4:
4.
Take my silver and my gold:
Not a mite would I withhold;
Take my intellect, and use
Every power as Thou shalt choose,
Every power as Thou shalt choose.
6:
6.
Take my love, my Lord, I pour
At Thy feet its treasure store;
Take myself and I will be
Ever, only, all for Thee,
Ever, only, all for Thee!

612.title: Take My Life, and Let It Be
612.credits:
Words: Frances R. Havergal
Music: Twelfth Mass, Attr.. Wolfgang A. Mozart

esp.title: Toma Mi Vida, Señor
esp.credits:
Palabras: Frances R. Havergal
Música: Twelfth Mass, Attr.. Wolfgang A. Mozart

1:
1.
Take my life, and let it be
Consecrated, Lord, to Thee;
Take my moments and my days,
Let them flow in ceaseless praise,
Let them flow in ceaseless praise.
2:
2.
Take my hands and let them move
At the impulse of Thy love;
Take my feet, and let them be
Swift and beautiful for Thee,
Swift and beautiful for Thee.
3:
3.
Take my voice, and let me sing
Always, only, for my King;
Take my lips, and let them be
Filled with messages from Thee,
Filled with messages from Thee.
4:
4.
Take my silver and my gold:
Not a mite would I withhold;
Take my intellect, and use
Every power as Thou shalt choose,
Every power as Thou shalt choose.
6:
6.
Take my love, my Lord, I pour
At Thy feet its treasure store;
Take myself and I will be
Ever, only, all for Thee,
Ever, only, all for Thee!

613.title: Take My Life, and Let It Be
613.credits:
Words: Frances R. Havergal
Music: McIntosh’s & Cunnyngham’s New Life, Arr.: Rigdon M. McIntosh

esp.title: Toma Mi Vida, Señor
esp.credits:
Palabras: Frances R. Havergal
Música: McIntosh’s & Cunnyngham’s New Life, Arr.: Rigdon M. McIntosh

1:
1.
That dreadful night before His death,
The Lamb for sinner's slain,
Did, almost with His dying breath
This solemn feast ordain.
2:
2.
To keep the feast, Lord, we have met,
And to remember Thee,
Help each redeemed one to repeat,
"For me, He died, for me!"

1:
1.
Sweet hour of prayer! Sweet hour of prayer,
That calls me from a world of care,
And bids me at my Father's throne
Make all my wants and wishes known;
In seasons of distress and grief 
My soul has often found relief,
And oft escaped the tempter's snare,
By thy return, sweet hour of prayer.
2:
2.
Sweet hour of prayer! Sweet hour of prayer,
The joy I feel, the bliss I share
Of those whose anxious spirits burn
With strong desires for thy return!
With such I hasten to the place
Where God, my Savior, shows His face,
And gladly take my station there,
And wait for thee, sweet hour of prayer.
3:
3.
Sweet hour of prayer! Sweet hour of prayer,
Thy wings shall my petition bear
To Him whose truth and faithfulness
Engage the waiting soul to bless;
And since He bids me seek His face,
Believe His word, and trust His grace,
I'll cast on Him my every care,
And wait for thee, sweet hour of prayer.

618.title: Sweet Hour Of Prayer
618.credits:
Words: William W. Walford
Music: William B. Bradbury

esp.title: Dulce Oración
esp.credits:
Palabras: William W. Walford
Música: William B. Bradbury

1:
1.
Teach me Thy way, O Lord, Teach me Thy way!
Thy guiding grace afford— Teach my Thy way!
Help me to walk aright,
More by faith, less by sight;
Lead me with heavenly light—
Teach me Thy way!
2:
2.
When I am sad at heart, Teach me Thy way!
When earthly joys depart, Teach me Thy way!
In hours of loneliness,
In times of dire distress,
In failure or success,
Teach me Thy way!
3:
3.
When doubts and fears arise, Teach me Thy way!
When storms over spread the skies, Teach me Thy way!
Shine through the cloud and rain,
Through sorrow, toil and pain;
Make Thou my pathway plain—
Teach me Thy way!
4:
4.
Long as my life shall last, Teach me Thy way!
Wherever my lot be cast, Teach me Thy way!
Until the race is run,
Until the journey's done,
Until the crown is won,
Teach me Thy way!

620.title: Teach Me Thy Way, O Lord

esp.title: Enséñame Tu Camino, Señor
esp.credits: Palabras y Música: B. Mansell Ramsey

1c:
He could have called ten thousand angels
To destroy the world and set Him free.
He could have called, ten thousand angels,
But He died alone, for you and me.
2:
2.
Upon His precious head they placed a crown of thorns;
They laughed and said, "Be-hold the King,"
They struck Him and they cursed Him and mocked His holy name.
All alone He suffered everything.
2c:
He could have called ten thousand angels
To destroy the world and set Him free.
He could have called, ten thousand angels,
But He died alone, for you and me.
3:
3.
When they nailed Him to the cross, His mother stood nearby;
He said, "Woman behold thy son!"
He cried, "I thirst” for water, but they gave Him none to drink.
Then the sinful work of man was done.
3c:
He could have called ten thousand angels
To destroy the world and set Him free.
He could have called, ten thousand angels,
But He died alone, for you and me.
4:
4.
To the howling mob He yielded;
He did not for mercy cry.
The cross of shame He took alone.
And when He cried, "It's finished,"
He gave Himself to die;
Salvation's wondrous plan was done.
4c:
He could have called ten thousand angels
To destroy the world and set Him free.
He could have called, ten thousand angels,
But He died alone, for you and me.

621.title: Ten Thousand Angels
621.credits:
Words and Music by: Ray Overholt
Copyright © 1959 Lillenas Publishing Company (Administered by The Copyright Company Nashville, TN)
All Rights Reserved. International Copyright Secured. Used By Permission.

esp.title: Diez Mil Ángeles
esp.credits:
Palabras y Musica por: Ray Overholt
Los Derechs De Autor © 1959 Lillenas Publishing Company (Administered by The Copyright Company Nashville, TN)
Todos Los Derechos Reservados. Derechos De Autor Internacionales Garantizados. Utilizada Con Permiso.

1:
1.
Tell me the story of Jesus, 
Write on my heart every word;
Tell me the story most precious, 
Sweetest that ever was heard;
Tell how the an-gels, in chorus, 
Sang as they welcomed His birth:
"Glory to God in the highest! 
Peace and good tidings on earth."
1c:
Tell me the story of Jesus, 
Write on my heart every word:
Tell me the story most precious, 
Sweetest that ever was heard.
2:
2.
Fasting alone in the desert, 
Tell of the days that are passed,
How for our sins He was tempted, 
Yet was triumphant at last;
Tell of the years of His labor, 
Tell of the sorrow He bore,
He was despised and afflict-ed, 
Homeless, rejected and poor:
2c:
Tell me the story of Jesus, 
Write on my heart every word:
Tell me the story most precious, 
Sweetest that ever was heard.
3:
3.
Tell of the cross where they nailed Him, 
Writhing in anguish and pain;
Tell of the grave where they laid Him, 
Tell how He liveth a-gain.
Love, in that story so tender, 
Clearer than ever I see;
Stay, let me weep while you whisper, 
"Love paid the ransom for me."
3c:
Tell me the story of Jesus, 
Write on my heart every word:
Tell me the story most precious, 
Sweetest that ever was heard.

622.title: Tell Me the Story of Jesus
622.credits:
Words by: Fanny J. Crosby
Music by: John R. Sweney

esp.title: Dime La Historia De Cristo
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Fanny J. Crosby
Musica por: John R. Sweney

2:
2.
Elect from every nation
Yet one o'er all the earth;  
Her charter of salvation,
One Lord, one faith, one birth;  
One holy name she blesses,
Partakes one holy food,
And to one hope she presses,
With every grace endued.
3:
3.
Though with a scornful wonder
Men see her sore oppressed,
By schisms rent asunder,
By heresies distressed,
Yet saints their watch are keeping,
Their cry goes up, "How long?"
And soon the night of weeping
Shall be the morn of song.
4:
4.
'Mid toil and tribulation, 
And tumult of her war,
She waits the consummation 
Of peace forever-more;
Till with the vision glorious, 
Her longing eyes are blessed,
And the great church victorious 
Shall be the church at rest.

624.title: The Church’s One Foundation
624.credits:
Words by: Samuel J. Stone
Music by: George J. Webb

esp.title: Cimiento De La Iglesia
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Samuel J. Stone
Música por: George J. Webb

1:
1.
Of one the Lord has made the race,
Through one has come the fall;
Where sin has gone must go His grace:
The gospel is for all.
1c:
The blessed gospel is for all
The gospel is for all;
Where sin has gone must go His grace:
The gospel is for all.
2:
2.
Say not the heathen are at home,
Beyond we have no call,
For why should we be blest alone?
The gospel is for all.
2c:
The blessed gospel is for all
The gospel is for all;
Where sin has gone must go His grace:
The gospel is for all.

3.
Received ye freely, freely give,

3c:
The blessed gospel is for all
The gospel is for all;
Where sin has gone must go His grace:
The gospel is for all.

1:
1.
The great Physician now is near,

Sweetest note in seraph song,
Sweetest name on mortal tongue,
Sweetest carol ever sung,
Jesus, blessed Jesus.
2:
2.
All glory to the dying Lamb! 
I now believe in Jesus;
I love the blessed Savior's name,
I love the name of Jesus.
2c:
Sweetest note in seraph song,
Sweetest name on mortal tongue,
Sweetest carol ever sung,
Jesus, blessed Jesus.
3:
3.
His name dispels my guilt and fear,
No other name but Jesus;
O how my soul delights to hear

3c:
Sweetest note in seraph song,
Sweetest name on mortal tongue,
Sweetest carol ever sung,
Jesus, blessed Jesus.
4:
4.
And when to that bright world above,

4c:
Sweetest note in seraph song,
Sweetest name on mortal tongue,

Jesus, blessed Jesus.

1:
1.
The mighty God, Omniscient One!
His ways we cannot trace.
He reckons every good begun 
And crowns it with His grace.
1c:
He's here, and there, and everywhere
In all the ways I've trod.
I've never passed beyond the sphere 
Of the providence of God.
2:
2.
Lo! I can see Him in His word—
I will not doubt or fear;
My steps are ordered of the Lord,
His guiding hand is near.
2c:
He's here, and there, and everywhere
In all the ways I've trod.
I've never passed beyond the sphere 
Of the providence of God.
3:
3.
No trial can my spirit break,
For God will not forsake;
He will with each temptation make
A way for my escape.
3c:
He's here, and there, and everywhere
In all the ways I've trod.
I've never passed beyond the sphere 
Of the providence of God.
4:
4.
The future beckons and I bow—
My God removes the care!
Behold, He goes before me now,
And will my way prepare.
4c:
He's here, and there, and everywhere
In all the ways I've trod.
I've never passed beyond the sphere 
Of the providence of God.

1:
1.
The Lord's my Shepherd; I'll not want:
He makes me down to lie

In pastures green, He leadeth me 
The quiet waters by.
2:
2.
My soul He doth restore again,
And me to walk doth make
Within the paths of righteousness,
Within the paths of righteousness,
Even for His own name's sake.
3:
3.
Yea, though I walk in death's dark vale,
Yet will I fear none ill;
For Thou art with me, and Thy rod,
For Thou art with me, and Thy rod

4:
4.
My table Thou hast furnished

My head Thou dost with oil anoint,
My head Thou dost with oil anoint,
And my cup overflows.
5:
5.
Goodness and mercy all my life

And in God's house for evermore,
My dwelling place shall be.

2.

644.title: ‘Tis Set, the Feast Divine
644.credits:
Words & Music: Lloyd O. Sanderson
Copyright © 1976 by Leon B. Sanderson

1:
1.
The love of God is greater far
Than tongue or pen can ever tell;
It goes be-yond the highest star,
And reaches to the lowest hell.

His erring child He reconciled,
And pardoned from his sin.
1c:
O love of God, how rich and pure!
How measureless and strong!
It shall forever-more endure—
The saints' and angels' song.
2c:
O love of God, how rich and pure!
How measureless and strong!
It shall forever-more endure—
The saints' and angels' song.
3c:
O love of God, how rich and pure!
How measureless and strong!
It shall forever-more endure—
The saints' and angels' song.

646.title: The Love of God
646.credits:
Words: Fredrick Martin Lehman
Music: Fredrick Martin Lehman / Arr.: Claudia Lehman Mays

1:
1.
The sands of time are sinking,
The dawn of heaven breaks;
The summer morn I've sighed for,
The fair, sweet morn awakes;
Dark, dark hath been the midnight,
But dayspring is at hand,
And glory, glory dwelleth
In Immanuel's land.
2:
2.
O Christ, He is the fountain,
The deep, sweet well of love;
The streams on earth I've tasted,
More deep I'll drink above;
There to an ocean fullness 
His mercy doth expand,
And glory, glory dwelleth
In Immanuel's land.
3:
3.
I've wrestled on towards heaven,
'Gainst storm, and wind, and tide;
Now, like a weary traveler,
That leaneth on his guide,
Amid the shades of evening,
While sinks life's lingering sand,
I hail the glory dawning,
In Immanuel's land.
4:
4.
With mercy and with judgment
My web of time He wove,
And aye the dews of sorrow
Were brightened by His love;
I'll bless the hand that guided,
I'll bless the heart that planned,
When throned where glory dwelleth
In Immanuel's land.
5:
5.
The bride eyes not her garment,
But her dear bridegroom's face;
I will not gaze at glory,
But on my King of grace;
Not at the crown He giveth,
But on His pierced hand:
The Lamb is all the glory
Of Immanuel's land.

650.title: The Sands of Time
650.credits:
Words: Anne Ross Cousin
Music: Chretien Urhan / Arr.: Edward F. Rimbault

1:
1.
There were ninety and nine that safely lay 
In the shelter of the fold,
But one was out on the hills away
Far off from the gates of gold;
Away on the mountains wild and bare,
Away from the tender Shepherd's care,
Away from the tender Shepherd's care.
2:
2.
"Lord, Thou hast here Thy ninety and nine,
Are they not enough for Thee?"
But the Shepherd made answer: "This of mine 
Has wandered away from me,
And although the road be rough and steep,
I go to the desert to find my sheep,
I go to the desert to find my sheep."
3:
3.
But none of the ransomed ever knew 
How deep were the waters crossed
Nor how dark was the night that the Lord passed through
Ere He found His sheep that was lost.
Far out in the desert He heard its cry,
'Twas sick and helpless and ready to die,
'Twas sick and helpless and ready to die.
4:
4.
"Lord, whence are those blood drops all the way
That marks out the mountain's track?"
"They were shed for one who had gone astray
Ere the Shepherd could bring him back."
"Lord, whence are Thy hands so rent and torn?
They're pierced tonight by many a thorn,
They're pierced tonight by many a thorn."
5:
5.
But all through the mountains, thunder-riven
And up from the rocky steep,
There arose a glad cry to the gate of heaven,
"Rejoice, I have found my sheep."
And the angels echoed around the throne,
"Rejoice, for the Lord brings back His own,
Rejoice, for the Lord brings back His own."

651.title: The Ninety and Nine
651.credits:
Words: Elizabeth C. Clephane

1:
1.
I must needs go home by the way of the cross:

1c:
The way of the cross leads home,
The way of the cross leads home;
It is sweet to know, as I onward go,
The way of the cross leads home.
2:
2.
I must needs go on in the blood-sprinkled way,
The path that the Savior trod,
If I ever climb to the heights sublime,
Where the soul is at home with God.
2c:
The way of the cross leads home,
The way of the cross leads home;
It is sweet to know, as I onward go,
The way of the cross leads home.
3:
3.
Then I bid farewell to the way of the world,
To walk in it nevermore;
For my Lord says, "Come", and I seek my home,
Where He waits at the open door.
3c:
The way of the cross leads home,
The way of the cross leads home;
It is sweet to know, as I onward go,
The way of the cross leads home.

3.

 There's a rock that's cleft and no soul is left,

4c:
Will you come to the fountain free?

3.

2.

3.

There is a fountain filled with blood,

2.

3.

3.

1:
1.
In the dark of the midnight have I oft hid my face,
While the storm howls above me, and there's no hiding place.
'Mid the crash of the thunder, precious Lord, hear my cry;
Keep me safe 'til the storm passes by.
1c:
'Til the storm passes over,
'Til the thunder sounds no more,
'Til the clouds roll forever from the sky,
Hold me fast, let me stand in the hollow of Thy hand.
Keep me safe 'til the storm passes by.
2:
2.
Many times Satan whispered, "There is no need to try,
For there's no end to sorrow, there's no hope by and by."
But I know Thou art with me, and tomorrow I'll rise
Where the storms never darken the skies.
2c:
'Til the storm passes over,
'Til the thunder sounds no more,
'Til the clouds roll forever from the sky,
Hold me fast, let me stand in the hollow of Thy hand.
Keep me safe 'til the storm passes by.
3:
3.
When the long night has ended and the storms come no more,
Let me stand in Thy presence on the bright peaceful shore.
In that land where the tempest never comes, Lord, may I
Dwell with Thee when the storm passes by.
3c:
'Til the storm passes over,
'Til the thunder sounds no more,
'Til the clouds roll forever from the sky,
Hold me fast, let me stand in the hollow of Thy hand.
Keep me safe 'til the storm passes by.

665.title: Till the Storm Passes By

All rights reserved. International copyright secured. Used by permission.

1:
1.
The spacious firmament on high,
With all the blue, ethereal sky,
And spangled heavens, a shining frame,
Their great Original proclaim:
The unwearied sun from day to day
Does his Creator's power display,
And publishes to every land
The work of an almighty hand.
2:
2.
Soon as the evening shades prevail,
The moon takes up the wondrous tale,
And nightly to the listening earth
Repeats the story of her birth;
While all the stars that round her burn,
And all the planets in their turn,
Confirm the tidings as they roll,
And spread the truth from pole to pole.
3:
3.
What though in solemn silence all
Move round this dark terrestrial ball?
What though no real voice nor sound
Amid their radiant orbs be found?
In reason's ear they all rejoice,
And utter forth a glorious voice,
For ever singing as they shine,
"The hand that made us is divine."

1:
1.
Would you be free from the burden of sin?
There's power in the blood, power in the blood;

There is power, power, Wonder-working power

In the precious blood of the Lamb.
2c:
There is power, power, Wonder-working power

There is power, power, Wonder-working power

There is power, power, Wonder-working power

4.

There's wonderful power in the blood.
4c:
There is power, power, Wonder-working power

esp.title: Hay Poder En La Sangre

1:
1. 
This is my Father's world, And to my listening ears, 
All nature sings, and round me rings 
The music of the spheres. 
This is my Father's world, I rest me in the thought 
Of rocks and trees of skies and seas; 
His hand the wonders wrought.
2:
2. 
This is my Father's world, 
The birds their carols raise; 
The morning light, the lily white 
Declare their Maker's praise. 
This is my Father's world, 
He shines in all that's fair; 
In the rustling grass I hear Him pass, 
He speaks to me everywhere.
3:
3. 
This is my Father's world, 
O let me ne'er forget 
That though the wrong seems oft so strong, 
God is the ruler yet. 
This is my Father's world, 
Why should my heart be sad? 
The Lord is King: Let heaven ring!
God reigns: Let earth be glad!

Marching on! marching on!
For Christ count everything but loss,
And to crown Him Kings, toil and sing

2:
3.
Over land and sea, wherever man may dwell,

2c:
Marching on! marching on!
For Christ count everything but loss,
And to crown Him Kings, toil and sing
Beneath the banner of the cross.
3:
3.
When the glory dawns— 'tis drawing very near,

3c:
Marching on! marching on!
For Christ count everything but loss,
And to crown Him Kings, toil and sing

m

Praise the Lord, praise the Lord,
Let the earth hear His voice!

3.

2c:

3c:

Jesus, Jesus, how I trust Him!

Jesus, Jesus, how I trust Him!

688.credits:
Words & Music: Lloyd O. Sanderson
Copyright © 1971 by L. O. Sanderson, Leon B. Sanderson, owner.
All rights reserved. Used by permission.

esp.title: Recuérdame Así
esp.credits:
Palabras y Múscia: Lloyd O. Sanderson
Los Derechos de Autor © 1971 by L. O. Sanderson, Leon B. Sanderson, dueño.
Todos Los Derechos Reservados. Utilizado Por El Permiso.

1:
1.
To Canaan's land I'm on my way
Where the soul (of man) never dies;
My darkest night will turn to day,
Where the soul (of man) never dies.
1c:
Dear friends, there'll be no sad fair-wells,
There'll be no tear-dimmed eyes,
Where all is peace and joy and love,
And the soul of man never dies.
2:
2.
A rose is blooming there for me,
Where the soul (of man) never dies;
And I will spend eternity,
Where the soul (of man) never dies.
2c:
Dear friends, there'll be no sad fair-wells,
There'll be no tear-dimmed eyes,
Where all is peace and joy and love,
And the soul of man never dies.
3:
3.
A love-light beams across the foam,
Where the soul (of man) never dies;
It shines to light the shores of home,
Where the soul (of man) never dies.
3c:
Dear friends, there'll be no sad fair-wells,
There'll be no tear-dimmed eyes,
Where all is peace and joy and love,
And the soul of man never dies.
4:
4.
My life will end in deathless sleep,
Where the soul (of man) never dies;
And everlasting joys I'll reap,
Where the soul (of man) never dies.
4c:
Dear friends, there'll be no sad fair-wells,
There'll be no tear-dimmed eyes,
Where all is peace and joy and love,
And the soul of man never dies.
5:
5.
I'm on my way to that fair land,
Where the soul (of man) never dies;
Where there will be no parting hand,
Where the soul (of man) never dies.
5c:
Dear friends, there'll be no sad fair-wells,
There'll be no tear-dimmed eyes,
Where all is peace and joy and love,
And the soul of man never dies.

694.title: To Canaan’s Land I’m on My Way
694.credits: Words and Music by: William M. Golden

1:
1.
This is the day the Lord hath made;
He calls the hours His own.
Let heaven rejoice, let earth be glad,
And praise surround the throne.
2:
2.
Today He rose and left the dead,
And Satan’s empire fell;
Today the saints His triumph spread,
And all His wonders tell.
3:
3.
Hosanna to the anointed King,
To David's holy Son!
Help us, O Lord! Descend and bring
Salvation from Thy throne.
4:
4.
Blest be the Lord, who comes to men
With messages of grace;
Who comes in God His Father's name,
To save our sinful race.

695.title: This Is the Day the Lord Hath Made
695.credits:
Words: Isaac Watts
Music: Thomas A. Arne / Arr.: Ralph Harrison.

1:
1.
We are called to be God's people,
Showing by our lives His grace.
One in heart and one in spirit,
Sign of hope for all the race.
Let us show how He has changed us,
And remade us as His own,
Let us share our life together
As we shall around His throne.
2:
2.
We are called to be God's servants,
Working in His world today;
Taking His own task upon us,
All His sacred words obey.
Let us rise, then, to His summons,
Dedicate to Him our all,
That we may be faithful servants,
Quick to answer now His call.
3:
3.
We are called to be God's prophets,
Spokesmen for the truth and right;
Standing firm for godly justice,
Bringing evil into light.
Let us seek the courage needed,
Our high calling to fulfill,
That mankind may know the blessing 
Of the doing of God's will.

706.title: We Are Called to Be God’s People
706.credits:
Words: Thomas A. Jackson / Music: Franz J. Haydn
Words Copyright © 1973, Ren. 2001 by Broadman Press (SESAC) (adm. by LifeWay Worship)
All rights reserved. Used by permission.

1:
1.
To Christ be loyal and be true;
His banner be unfurled,
And borne aloft till is secured
The conquest of the world.
1c:
To Christ the Lord be true,
For He will go with you,
And help you all your conflicts through;
To Christ the Lord be true.
2:
2.
To Christ be loyal and be true;
He needs brave volunteers
To stand against the powers of sin,
Moved not by frowns or fears.
2c:
To Christ the Lord be true,
For He will go with you,
And help you all your conflicts through;
To Christ the Lord be true.
3:
3.
To Christ be loyal and be true;
In noble service prove
Your faith and your fidelity,
The fervor of your love.
3c:
To Christ the Lord be true,
For He will go with you,
And help you all your conflicts through;
To Christ the Lord be true.
4:
4.
To Christ be loyal and be true;
And He will be your friend,
Defending and protecting you
To life's triumph-ant end.
4c:
To Christ the Lord be true,
For He will go with you,
And help you all your conflicts through;
To Christ the Lord be true.

707.title: To Christ Be Loyal and Be True
707.credits:
Words by: Elisha A. Hoffman
Music by: D. M. Wilson

esp.title: A Jesucristo Sé Fiel
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Elisha A. Hoffman
Musica por: D. M. Wilson

1:
1. True-hearted, whole-hearted, faithful and loyal,
King of our lives, by Thy grace we will be;
Under the standard exalted and royal,
Strong in Thy strength we will battle for Thee.
1c:
Peal out the watchword! 
Silence it never!
Song of our spirits,
rejoicing and free;
Peal out the watchword!
Loyal for ever,
King of our lives,
by Thy grace we will be.
2:
2. True-hearted, whole-hearted, fullest allegiance
Yielding henceforth to our glorious King;
Valiant endeavor and loving obedience,
Freely and joyously now would we bring.
2c:
Peal out the watchword! 
Silence it never!
Song of our spirits,
rejoicing and free;
Peal out the watchword!
Loyal for ever,
King of our lives,
by Thy grace we will be.
3:
3. True-hearted, whole-hearted, Savior all-glorious!
Take Thy great power and reign there alone,
Over our wills and affections victorious,
Freely surrendered and wholly Thine own.
3c:
Peal out the watchword! 
Silence it never!
Song of our spirits,
rejoicing and free;
Peal out the watchword!
Loyal for ever,
King of our lives,
by Thy grace we will be.

711.title: True-Hearted, Whole-Hearted
711.credits:
Words: Frances R. Havergal
Music: George C. Stebbins

esp.title: Corazón Verdadero Y Sincero
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Frances R. Havergal
Musica por: George C. Stebbins

1:
1.
When we walk with the Lord
In the light of His Word,
What a glory He sheds on our way!  
While we do His good will,
He abides with us still,
And with all who will trust and obey.
1c:
Trust and obey,
for there's no other way
To be happy in Jesus,
but to trust and obey.
2:
2.
Not a shadow can rise,
Not a cloud in the skies,
But His smile quickly drives it away;  
Not a doubt nor a fear,
Not a sigh nor a tear,
Can abide while we trust and obey.
2c:
Trust and obey,
for there's no other way
To be happy in Jesus,
but to trust and obey.
3:
3.
Not a burden we bear,
Not a sorrow we share,
But our toil He doth richly repay;  
Not a grief nor a loss,
Not a frown nor a cross,
but is blest if we trust and obey.
3c:
Trust and obey,
for there's no other way
To be happy in Jesus,
but to trust and obey.
4:
4.
But we never can prove
The delights of His love
Until all on the altar we lay;  
For the favor He shows,
And the joy He bestows,
Are for those who will trust and obey.
4c:
Trust and obey,
for there's no other way
To be happy in Jesus,
but to trust and obey.
5:
5.
Then in fellowship sweet,
We will sit at His feet,
Or we'll walk by His side in the way;  
What He says we will do,
Where He sends we will go
Never fear, only trust and obey.
5c:
Trust and obey,
for there's no other way
To be happy in Jesus,
but to trust and obey.

714.title: Trust and Obey
714.credits:
Words by: J. H. Sammis
Music by: D. B. Towner

esp.title: Cuando Andemos Con Dios
esp.credits:
Palabras por: J. H. Sammis
Musica por: D. B. Towner

1:
1.
I heard an old, old story, How a Savior came from glory,
How He gave his life on Calvary To save a wretch like me;
I heard about His groaning, Of His precious blood's atoning,
Then I repented of my sins And won the victory.
1c:
O victory in Jesus, my Savior, forever,
He sought me and bought me with His redeeming blood;
He loved me ere I knew Him, and all my love is due Him,
He plunged me to victory, beneath the cleansing flood.
2:
2.
I heard about His healing, Of His cleansing power revealing,
How He made the lame to walk again And caused the blind to see;
And then, I cried, "Dear Jesus, Come and heal my broken spirit,"
I then obeyed His blest commands And gained the victory.
2c:
O victory in Jesus, my Savior, forever,
He sought me and bought me with His redeeming blood;
He loved me ere I knew Him, and all my love is due Him,
He plunged me to victory, beneath the cleansing flood.
3:
3.
I heard about a mansion He has built for me in glory,
And I heard about the streets of gold Beyond the crystal sea;
About the angels singing, And the old redemption story,
And some sweet day I'll sing up there The song of victory.
3c:
O victory in Jesus, my Savior, forever,
He sought me and bought me with His redeeming blood;
He loved me ere I knew Him, and all my love is due Him,
He plunged me to victory, beneath the cleansing flood.

717.title: Victory in Jesus
717.credits:
Words and Music by: E. M. Bartlett
Copyright © 1939 by E.M. Bartlett. Copyright 1967 by Mrs. E.M. Bartlett, renewal.
Assigned to Albert E. Brumley & Sons/SESAC (admin. By ICG).
All rights reserved. Used by permission.

esp.title: Victoria En Cristo
esp.credits:
Palabras y Música por: E. M. Bartlett
Los Derechos de Autor © 1939 by E.M. Bartlett. Los Derechos de Autor 1967 by Mrs. E.M. Bartlett, renewal.
Assigned to Albert E. Brumley & Sons/SESAC (admin. By ICG).
Todos los Derechos Reservados. Utilizado por permiso.

1:
1. 
We plow the fields and scatter
The good seed on the land.
But it is fed and watered
By God's almighty hand.
He sends the snow in winter,
The warmth to swell the grains,
The breezes and the sunshine,
And soft refreshing rain.
2:
2. 
He only is the Maker
Of all things near and far.
He paints the wayside flowers,
He lights the evening star.
The wind and waves obey Him.
By Him the birds are fed;
Much more to us His children
He gives our daily bread.
3:
3. 
We thank Thee, then, O Father,
For all things bright and good;
The seed time and the harvest,
Our life, our health, our food.
Accept the gifts we offer,
For all Thy love imparts,
And, what Thou most desirest,
Our humble, thankful hearts.

725.title: We Plow the Fields
725.credits:
Words: Matthias Claudius / tr. Jane Montgomery Alexander / Music: R. J. Stevens
Music Copyright © 1986 by Shepherd-Stevens Music, Inc., PO Box 665, Kemp, TX 75143,
in Hymns for Worship. All rights reserved. Used by permission.

1:
1.
We saw Thee not when Thou didst come
To this poor world of sin and death;  
Nor yet beheld Thy cottage home,
In that despised Nazareth;  
But we believe Thy footsteps trod
Its streets and plains, Thou Son of God:
But we believe Thy footsteps trod
Its streets and plains, Thou Son of God.
2:
2.
We saw Thee not when lifted high,
Amid that wild and savage crew;  
Nor heard we that imploring cry,
"Forgive, they know not what they do!"
But we believe the deed was done,
That shook the earth and veiled the sun;  
But we believe the deed was done,
That shook the earth and veiled the sun.
3:
3.
We gazed not in the open tomb,
Where once Thy mangled body lay;  
Nor saw Thee in that "upper room,"
Nor met Thee on the open way;  
But we believe that angels said,
"Why seek the living with the dead?"
But we believe that angels said,
"Why seek the living with the dead?"

726.title: We Saw Thee Not
726.credits:
Words by: Anne Richter / arr.: John H. Gurney
Music by: Knowles Shaw

esp.title: No Te Vimos
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Anne Richter / arr.: John H. Gurney
Musica por: Knowles Shaw

1:
1.
Though the way we journey may be often drear,
We shall see the King some day, 
On that blessed morning clouds will disappear:
We shall see the King some day.
1c:
We shall see the King some day,
We will shout and sing some day;
Gathered round the throne, when He shall call His own,
We shall see the King some day.
2:
2.
After pain and anguish, after toil and care,
We shall see the King some day, 
Through the endless ages joy and blessing share:
We shall see the King some day.
2c:
We shall see the King some day,
We will shout and sing some day;
Gathered round the throne, when He shall call His own,
We shall see the King some day.
3:
3.
After foes are conquered, after battles won,
We shall see the King some day, 
After strife is over, after set of sun,
We shall see the King some day.
3c:
We shall see the King some day,
We will shout and sing some day;
Gathered round the throne, when He shall call His own,
We shall see the King some day.
4:
4.
There with all the loved ones who have gone before,
We shall see the King some day, 
Sorrow past for ever, on that peaceful shore,
We shall see the King some day.
4c:
We shall see the King some day,
We will shout and sing some day;
Gathered round the throne, when He shall call His own,
We shall see the King some day.

727.title: We Shall See the King Some Day

1:
1.
What a Friend we have in Jesus,
All our sins and griefs to bear;
What a privilege to carry
Everything to God in prayer.
O what peace we often forfeit,
O what needless pain we bear,
All because we do not carry
Everything to God in prayer.
2:
2.
Have we trials and temptations?
Is there trouble anywhere?
We should never be discouraged,
Take it to the Lord in prayer.
Can we find a friend so faithful,
Who will all our sorrows share?
Jesus knows our every weakness:
Take it to the Lord in prayer.
3:
3.
Are we weak and heavy-laden,
Cumbered with a load of care?
Precious Savior, still our refuge,
Take it to the Lord in prayer.
Do thy friends despise, forsake thee?
Take it to the Lord in prayer;
In His arms He'll take and shield thee,
Thou wilt find a solace there.

730.title: What a Friend We Have in Jesus
730.credits:
Words: Joseph Scriven
Music: Charles C. Converse

1:
1.
We Praise Thee, O God, 
For the Son of Thy love,
For Jesus who died and is now gone above.
1c:
Hallelujah! Thine the glory,
Hallelujah! Amen!
Hallelujah! Thine the glory,
Revive us again.
2:
2.
We praise Thee, O God,
For Thy Spirit of light,
Who has shown us our Savior, 
and scattered our night.
2c:
Hallelujah! Thine the glory,
Hallelujah! Amen!
Hallelujah! Thine the glory,
Revive us again.
3:
3.
All glory and praise
To the Lamb that was slain,
Who has borne all our sins, 
and has cleansed every stain.
3c:
Hallelujah! Thine the glory,
Hallelujah! Amen!
Hallelujah! Thine the glory,
Revive us again.
4:
4.
All glory and praise
To the God of all grace,
Who has brought us and sought us and guided our ways.
4c:
Hallelujah! Thine the glory,
Hallelujah! Amen!
Hallelujah! Thine the glory,
Revive us again.
5:
5.
Revive us again:
Fill each heart with Thy love;
May each soul be rekindled with fire from above.
5c:
Hallelujah! Thine the glory,
Hallelujah! Amen!
Hallelujah! Thine the glory,
Revive us again.

732.title: We Praise Thee, O God
732.credits:
Words by: William P. Mackay
Music by: J. J. Husband

esp.title: Te Loamos Oh Dios
esp.credits:
Palabras por: William P. Mackay
Musica por: J. J. Husband

1:
1.
We will glorify the King of kings, 
We will glorify the Lamb;
We will glorify the Lord of lords,
Who is the great I AM.
2:
2. 
Lord Jehovah reigns in majesty,
We will bow before His throne;
We will worship Him in righteousness,
We worship Him alone.
3:
3. 
He is Lord of heaven, Lord of earth,
He is Lord of all who live;
He is Lord above the universe,
All praise to Him we give.
4:
4. 
Hallelujah to the King of kings,
Hallelujah to the Lamb;
Hallelujah to the Lord of lords,
Who is the great I AM.

738.title: We Will Glorify
738.credits:
Words and Music by: Twila Paris
Copyright © 1982 by Singspiration Music(ASCAP)(admin. by Brentwood-Benson Music Publishing, Inc.)
All Rights reserved. Used by permission.

esp.title: Adoremos
esp.credits:
Palabras y Musica por: Twila Paris
Copyright © 1982 by Singspiration Music(ASCAP)(admin. by Brentwood-Benson Music Publishing, Inc.)
Todos los derechos reservados. Utilizada con permiso.

1:
1.
What will you do with Jesus?
The question comes to you!
And you must give an answer,
For something you must do.
1c:
What shall it be?
What shall it be?
What shall your answer be?
What will you do with Jesus?
O what shall your answer be?
2:
2.
What will you do with Jesus?
It comes by night and day;
With pierced hands uplifted,
He waits- what will you say?
2c:
What shall it be?
What shall it be?
What shall your answer be?
What will you do with Jesus?
O what shall your answer be?
3:
3.
What will you do with Jesus?
He's knocking at the door!
Refuse Him, soul, no longer,
Lest He should plead no more.
3c:
What shall it be?
What shall it be?
What shall your answer be?
What will you do with Jesus?
O what shall your answer be?

739.title: What Will You Do with Jesus?
739.credits:
Words: James Robinson
Music: Bentley D. Ackley

esp.title: ¿Qué Vas A Hacer Con Jesús?
esp.credits:
Palabras: James Robinson
Música: Bentley D. Ackley

1:
1.
When I survey the wondrous cross
On which the Prince of glory died,
My richest gain I count but loss
And pour contempt on all my pride.
2:
2.
Forbid it, Lord, that I should boast,
Save in the death of Christ, my Lord;  
All the vain things that charm me most
I sacrifice them to His blood.
3:
3.
See, from His head, His hands, His feet,
Sorrow and love flow mingled down;  
Did e'er such love and sorrow meet,
Or thorns compose so rich a crown?
4:
4.
His dying crimson, like a robe,
Spreads o'er His body on the tree;
Then am I dead to all the globe,
And all the globe is dead to me.
5:
5.
Were the whole realm of nature mine,
That were a present far too small;  
Love so amazing, so divine,
Demands my soul, my life, my all.

742.title: When I Survey the Wondrous Cross
742.credits:
Words by: Isaac Watts
Music by: Gregorian Chant, Arrangement by: Lowell Mason

esp.title: La Cruz Excelsa A Contemplar
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Isaac Watts
Musica por: Gregorian Chant, Arrangement by: Lowell Mason

1:
1.
When my love to Christ grows weak,
When for deeper faith I seek,
Then in thought I go to thee,
Garden of Gethsemane!
2:
2.
There I walk amid the shades,
While the lingering twilight fades,
See that suffering, friendless One,
Weeping, praying there alone.
3:
3.
When my love for man grows weak,
When for stronger faith I seek,
Hill of Calvary!  I go
To thy scenes of fear and woe.
4:
4.
There behold His agony,
Suffered on the bitter tree;  
See His anguish, see His faith
Love triumphant still in death.
5:
5.
Then to life I turn again,
Learning all the worth of pain,
Learning all the might that lies
In a full self sacrifice.

752.title: When My Love to Christ Grows Weak
752.credits:
Words by: John R. Wreford
Music by: Phoebe Palmer Knapp

esp.title: Si Mi Amor Por Cristo Es
esp.credits:
Palabras por: John R. Wreford
Música por: Phoebe Palmer Knapp

1:
1.
When the trumpet of the Lord shall sound 
and time shall be no more,
And the morning breaks eternal, bright and fair;
When the saved of earth shall gather over on the other shore,
And the roll is called up yonder, I'll be there.
1c:
When the roll is called up yonder, 
When the roll is called up yonder, 
When the roll is called up yonder, 
When the roll is called up yonder, I'll be there.
2:
2.
On that bright and cloudless morning 
when the dead in Christ shall rise,
And the glory of His resurrection share;
When His chosen ones shall gather to their home beyond the skies,
And the roll is called up yonder, I'll be there.
2c:
When the roll is called up yonder, 
When the roll is called up yonder, 
When the roll is called up yonder, 
When the roll is called up yonder, I'll be there.
3:
3.
Let us labor for the Master from the dawn till setting sun, 
Let us talk of all His wondrous love and care;
Then when all of life is over and our work on earth is done,
And the roll is called up yonder, I'll be there.
3c:
When the roll is called up yonder, 
When the roll is called up yonder, 
When the roll is called up yonder, 
When the roll is called up yonder, I'll be there.

755.title: When the Roll Is Called Up Yonder

1:
1.
Sing the wondrous love of Jesus,
Sing His mercy and His grace:
In the mansions bright and blessed,
He'll prepare for us a place.
1c:
When we all get to heaven,
What a day of rejoicing that will be!
When we all see Jesus,
We'll sing and shout the victory.
2:
2.
While we walk the pilgrim pathway,
Clouds will overspread the sky;  
But when traveling days are over,
Not a shadow, not a sigh.
2c:
When we all get to heaven,
What a day of rejoicing that will be!
When we all see Jesus,
We'll sing and shout the victory.
3:
3.
Let us then be true and faithful,
Trusting, serving every day;  
Just one glimpse of Him in glory
Will the toils of life repay.
3c:
When we all get to heaven,
What a day of rejoicing that will be!
When we all see Jesus,
We'll sing and shout the victory.
4:
4.
Onward to the prize before us!
Soon His beauty we'll behold;
Soon the pearly gates will open,
We shall tread the streets of gold.
4c:
When we all get to heaven,
What a day of rejoicing that will be!
When we all see Jesus,
We'll sing and shout the victory.

756.title: When We All Get to Heaven
756.credits:
Words by: Eliza E. Hewitt
Music by: Emily D. Wilson

esp.title: Cantan Del Amor De Cristo
esp.credits:
Palabras por: Eliza E. Hewitt
Musica por: Emily D. Wilson

1:
1.
When this passing world is done,
When has sunk yon glaring sun,
When I stand with Christ on high,
Looking o'er life's history—
Then, Lord, shall I fully know,
Not till then, how much I owe.
2:
2.
When I stand before the throne,
Dressed in beauty not my own,
When I see Thee as Thou art,
Love Thee with unsinning heart—
Then, Lord, shall I fully know,
Not till then, how much I owe.
3:
3.
When the praise of heaven I hear,
Loud as thunders to the ear,
Loud as many waters' noise,
Sweet as harp's melodious voice—
Then, Lord, shall I fully know,
Not till then, how much I owe.

758.title: When This Passing World Is Done
758.credits:
Words: Robert M. McCheyne
Music: Spanish Air / Arr.: Benjamin Carr

esp.title: Cuando El Mundo Ya No Esté
esp.credits:
Palabras: Robert M. McCheyne
Musica: Spanish Air / Arr.: Benjamin Carr

1:
1. 
Where charity and love prevail
There God is ever found;
Brought here together by Christ's love,
By love are we thus bound.
2:
2. 
With grateful joy and holy fear
His charity we learn;
Let us with heart and mind and soul
Now love Him in return.
3:
3. 
Forgive we now each other's faults
As we our faults confess,
And let us love each other well
In Christian holiness.
4:
4. 
Let strife among us be unknown,
Let all contention cease;
Be His the glory that we see,
Be ours His holy peace.
5:
5. 
Let us recall that in our midst
Dwells God's begotten Son;
As members of His body joined,
We are in Him made one.

759.title: Where Charity and Love Prevail
759.credits:
Words: Latin Hymn / Trans.: Omer Westenforf / Music: Lucius Chapin
Translation Copyright © 1961 by World Library Publications, Inc. All rights reserved. Used by permission.

1:
1.
Who will follow Jesus, 
Standing for the right,
Holding up His banner
In the thickest fight?
Listening for His orders,
Ready to obey,
Who will follow Jesus, 
Serving Him today?
1c:
Who will follow Jesus?
Who will make reply, 
"I am on the Lord's side;
Master, here am I"?
Who will follow Jesus?
Who will make reply, 
"I am on the Lord's side;
Master, here am I"?

1:
1.
Will your anchor hold in the storms of life,
When the clouds unfold their wings of strife?  
When the strong tides lift, and the cables strain,
Will your anchor drift, or firm remain?
1c:
We have an anchor that keeps the soul
Steadfast and sure while the billows roll,
Fastened to the Rock which cannot move,
Grounded firm and deep in the Savior's love.
2:
2.
It is safely moored, 'twill the storm withstand,
For 'tis well secured by the Savior's hand;  
And the cables, passed from His heart to mine,

2c:
We have an anchor that keeps the soul
Steadfast and sure while the billows roll,
Fastened to the Rock which cannot move,
Grounded firm and deep in the Savior's love.
3:
3.

3c:
We have an anchor that keeps the soul
Steadfast and sure while the billows roll,
Fastened to the Rock which cannot move,
Grounded firm and deep in the Savior's love.
4:
4.

4c:
We have an anchor that keeps the soul
Steadfast and sure while the billows roll,

Grounded firm and deep in the Savior's love.
5:

We have an anchor that keeps the soul
