Shout the God-breathed prophecy:
"He who was will ever be!"
Kings of earth have passed away!
The Son is Lord this day!
3: Night to night I come to Him,
Kneel before His diadem;
While a thousand thousand sing,
I fall before the King.
Soon will He be changing me:
Clothed in immortality,
Swallowed up in victory,
And evermore to be.

088.title: Theophany (High above the Seraphim)
088.credits:
Theophany
© 2001 David and Jimona Maravilla
Tune: MAZURKA • Frederic Chopin (1829); arr. C. A. Roberts (2001)
Hymn: C. A. Roberts (2001)

esp.title: Teofanía (Más Alto Que Un Serafín)
esp.credits:
Teofanía
© 2001 David and Jimona Maravilla
Muscia por: MAZURKA • Frederic Chopin (1829); arr. C. A. Roberts (2001)
Palabras por: C. A. Roberts (2001)

1: You are my strength, You are my shield,
You are my help, my deliv'rer.
You are my rock, You are my hope,
You are my all, my Redeemer.
2: You are my light, You are my guide,
You are the song I am singing.
You are my smile, You are my peace,
You are the joy of my living.

091.title: You Are My Strength
091.credits:
© 1999 Glenda B. Schales
Tune: Glenda B. Schales (1999)
Hymn: Glenda B. Schales (1999)

esp.title: Eres Mi Fuerza
esp.credits:
© 1999 Glenda B. Schales
Musica: Glenda B. Schales (1999)
Palabras: Glenda B. Schales (1999)

1: Draw from the springs of salvation;
Give thanks to His great and holy name.
Make known His deeds among the people;
Make known His exalted way.
2: Call on His name with thanksgiving;
Yes, joyously praise His name in song.
Through love He authored our salvation;
Through love He did give His Son.
chorus: Praise the LORD and shout for joy,
For the Holy One is in our midst.
Praise the LORD and shout for joy,
For He is in our midst.
Praise the LORD and shout and cry for joy,
For the Holy One is in our midst.
Praise the LORD and shout and cry for joy,
For He is in our midst.

105.title: He Is in Our Midst
105.credits:
© 1982 Glenda B. Schales
Tune: Glenda B. Schales (1982)
Hymn: Glenda B. Schales (1982)

esp.title: Él Está Entre Nosotros
esp.credits:
© 1982 Glenda B. Schales
Musica: Glenda B. Schales (1982)
Palabras: Glenda B. Schales (1982)

1: Alleluia! Alleluia!
Alleluia! Alleluia!
Alleluia! Alleluia!
Alleluia! Alleluia!
2: Every day, Lord, O I need You
In my home, Lord, Alleluia!
Every day, Lord, O I need You;
I am Yours, Lord! Alleluia!
3: What a friend we have in Jesus,
All our sins and griefs to bear;
What a privilege to carry
Everything to God in prayer.
4: He's my Savior, my Redeemer -
How He loves me; how I love Him.
He is risen! He is coming!
Lord, come quickly! Alleluia!

111.title: Alleluia!
111.credits:
© 1972 Manna Music; arr. © 1986 Dane K. Shepard
Tune: CALAIS • Jerry Sinclair (1972); arr. Dane K. Shepard (1987)
           st. 4 Lancaster and Mabry's Rejoice! And Sing to the Lord (1974)
Hymn: Jerry Sinclair (1972); st. 3 Joseph M. Scriven (1855);

esp.title: ¡Aleluya!
esp.credits:
© 1972 Manna Music; arr. © 1986 Dane K. Shepard
Musica: CALAIS • Jerry Sinclair (1972); arr. Dane K. Shepard (1987)
           st. 4 Lancaster and Mabry's Rejoice! And Sing to the Lord (1974)
Palabras: Jerry Sinclair (1972); st. 3 Joseph M. Scriven (1855);

1: Jesus, the very thought of Thee
With sweetness fills my breast;
But sweeter far Thy face to see,
And in Thy presence rest.
2: O Jesus, light of all below,
Thou fount of living fire,
Surpassing all the joys we know
And all we can desire.
3: O hope of every contrite heart,
O joy of all the meek,
To those who fall, how kind Thou art!
How good to those who seek!
4: Jesus, our only joy be Thou,
As Thou our prize wilt be;
Jesus, be Thou our glory now,
And through eternity.

128.title: Jesus, the Very Thought of Thee
128.credits:
Tune: ST. AGNES • John B. Dykes (1866)
Hymn: Latin Hymn (1150); tr. Edward Caswall (1849)

1: Dawn and sunset, fierce and joyful,
Each reflects His mighty ways.
With the sea and sky before me,
I will give Him all my praise.
2: Stars will joy in praises from me;
"Lesser light" will know my voice.
When I give my God His glory,
Night will hear me and rejoice.
3: Shout His glory, brothers, sisters;
Laud His name and do His will.
Like the sands upon the shoreline
Are the praises due Him still.
chorus: I will wake the dawn with praises!
I will speak His name abroad.
I will worship Him forever,
He my Lord, my only God.

143.title: I Will Wake the Dawn with Praises
143.credits:
© 2001 Sarah J. Fuhrman and Glenda B. Schales
Tune: Glenda B. Schales (2001)
Hymn: Sarah J. Fuhrman (2001)

1: I love You, Lord, and I lift my voice
To worship You. O my soul, rejoice!
Take joy, my King, in what You hear;
May it be a sweet, sweet sound in Your ear.

144.title: I Love You, Lord
144.credits:
© 1978 House of Mercy Music (admin. by Maranatha! Music/Brentwood-Benson Music Publishing, Inc.)
Tune: Laurie Klein (1978); arr. Stevens and Shepard's Hymns for Worship Supplement (2007)
Hymn: Laurie Klein (1978)

1: Magnify, O magnify!
O magnify our God and Father.
Magnify, O magnify!
O magnify our God.
He has made us in His glorious image;
We ever strive to be like Him.
He alone is holy,
He alone is righteous;
Cry aloud with gladness, our joy in Him.
Cry aloud with gladness, our joy in Him.
2: Magnify, O magnify!
O magnify our God and Father.
Magnify, O magnify!
O magnify our God.
He has given us His Son to save us,
Now reconciled to be like Him.
Give Him praise forever,
Honor and revere Him,
Cry aloud with gladness, our joy in Him.
Cry aloud with gladness, our joy in Him.
3: Magnify, O magnify!
O magnify our God and Father.
Magnify, O magnify!
O magnify our God.
He will bring us to His wondrous glory,
To perfect fellowship with Him.
We will see His wonder,
We will see His beauty;
Cry aloud with gladness, our joy in Him.
Cry aloud with gladness, our joy in Him.

157.title: Magnify, O Magnify!
157.credits:
© 2000 Glenda B. Schales
Tune: Glenda B. Schales (2000)
Hymn: Glenda B. Schales (2000)

esp.title: ¡Magnifica A Nuestro Dios!
esp.credits:
© 2000 Glenda B. Schales
Música: Glenda B. Schales (2000)
Palabras: Glenda B. Schales (2000)

1: Jesus, Lamb of God,
Worthy is Your name;
Jesus, Lamb of God,
Worthy is Your name!
2: Jesus, Lamb of God,
Worthy is Your name;
Jesus, Lamb of God,
Worthy is Your name!
3: Jesus, Lamb of God,
Worthy is Your name;
Jesus, Lamb of God,
Worthy is Your name!

180.title: You Are My All in All
180.credits:
© 1991 Shepherd's Heart Music (admin. by Praisecharts Publishing, Inc.)
Tune: Dennis Jernigan (1991)
Hymn: Dennis Jernigan (1991)

1: "I am the Way."
"I am the Truth."
"I am the Life."
"I am the Light."
"I am the Son."
"I am the Lord."
"I am the King."
"I am the Christ."
2: "I am the Bread."
"I am the Vine."
"I am the First."
"I am the Last."
"Believe in Me."
"Be not afraid."
"Come unto Me."
"I am the Way."

194.title: I Am the Way
194.credits:
© 2004 Larry and Doris Bailey
Tune: C. A. Roberts (2004)
Hymn: C. A. Roberts (2004)

1: Known before the world;
Seen in future days:
Glimpsed by righteous men:
Son of God.
Sent as David's heir;
Named an ageless king
Granted all the earth:
Exalted.
2: Seen with blinded eyes;
Heard by heedless ears:
Met with wicked hearts:
Son of God.
Worshiped with contempt;
Crowned with blood and thorns
Throned upon a cross:
Exalted.
3: Scorned by those who watched;
Mocked by all the wise:
Loved within belief:
Son of God.
Born to take my death:
Slain to give me life:
Jesus, Son of God.
Exalted.
Exalted.

198.title: Exalted
198.credits:
© 1999 M. W. Bassford and C. E. Couchman 
Tune: EXALTED • C. E. Couchman (1999)
Hymn: M. W. Bassford (1999)

esp.title: Realzado
esp.credits:
© 1999 M. W. Bassford and C. E. Couchman 
Muscia: EXALTED • C. E. Couchman (1999)
Palabras: M. W. Bassford (1999)

1: Jesus, name above all names:
Beautiful Savior, glorious Lord,
Emmanuel, God is with us,
Blessed Redeemer, Living Word.
2: Jesus, LORD God Almighty,
Wonderful Couns'lor, Light of the world,
The Prince of Peace, Hope of glory,
Man of Sorrows, Lamb of God.

199.title: Jesus, Name above All Names
199.credits:
© 1974, 1978 Scripture in Song (admin. by Maranatha! Music/Brentwood-Benson Music Publishing, Inc.)
Tune: HEARN • Naida Hearn (1974); Naida Hearn (1978); arr. Stevens and Shepard's Hymns for Worship (1987)
Hymn: Naida Hearn (1974); optional st. 2 Dane K. Shepard (1987)

esp.title: Jesús, Nombre Sublime
esp.credits:
© 1974, 1978 Scripture in Song (admin. by Maranatha! Music/Brentwood-Benson Music Publishing, Inc.)
Música: HEARN • Naida Hearn (1974); Naida Hearn (1978); arr. Stevens and Shepard's Hymns for Worship (1987)
Palabras: Naida Hearn (1974); optional st. 2 Dane K. Shepard (1987)

1: "What shall I say? What shall I say?"
"Father, save Me from this hour?"
"What shall I say? What shall I say?"
"For this purpose I have come."
2: "I come to Thee. I come to Thee."
"I am no more in the world."
"I come to Thee. I come to Thee."
"Father, glorify Thy Son."

223.title: Soliloquy and Prayer
223.credits:
© 2002 Thomas and Charlotte Couchman
Tune: SHEKINAH • C. A. Roberts (2002)
Hymn: arr. C. A. Roberts (2002)

esp.title: Soliloquio Y Oración
esp.credits:
© 2002 Thomas and Charlotte Couchman
Muscia: SHEKINAH • C. A. Roberts (2002)
Palabras: arr. C. A. Roberts (2002)

1: He carried my sorrows,
He bore my griefs,
Was pierced for transgression,
Afflicted for peace.
2: He suffered in anguish,
He writhed in pain,
Was smitten, forsaken,
Abandoned and slain.
3: Despised and rejected,
He knew no sin,
Was crushed for His people,
No violence within.
4: My heart mourns His chast'ning,
My tears still fall,
My sin is the reason
He gave me His all.
chorus: He knew by His stripes I am healed,
Through His blood I can kneel,
For by His oppression
I worship my King.

229.title: He Carried My Sorrows
229.credits:
© 1993 Glenda B. Schales
Tune: Glenda B. Schales (1993); arr. R. J. Stevens (1993)
Hymn: Glenda B. Schales (1993)

esp.title: Él Llevó Mis Penas
esp.credits:
© 1993 Glenda B. Schales
Música: Glenda B. Schales (1993); arr. R. J. Stevens (1993)
Palabras: Glenda B. Schales (1993)

1: Cross of Jesus, cross of sorrow,
Where the blood of Christ was shed,
Perfect Man on thee did suffer,
Perfect God on thee has bled!
2: From the "Holy, Holy, Holy,
We adore Thee, O Most High,"
Down to earth's blaspheming voices
And the shout of "Crucify."
3: Once the Lord of brilliant seraphs,
Winged with love to do His will,
Now the scorn of all His creatures,
And the aim of every ill.
4: Here the King of all the ages,
Throned in light ere worlds could be,
Robed in mortal flesh, is dying,
Crucified by sin for me.
5: Who shall fathom that descending
From the rainbow-circled throne,
Down to earth's most base profaning,
Dying desolate alone.
6: By His passion we can plead;
God has borne all mortal anguish;
Surely He will know our need.

231.title: Cross of Jesus, Cross of Sorrow
231.credits:
Tune: ST. SYLVESTER • John B. Dykes (1862)
Hymn: William J. Sparrow-Simpson (1887)

esp.title: Cruz De Jesús, Cruz De Pena
esp.credits:
Música: ST. SYLVESTER • John B. Dykes (1862)
Palabras: William J. Sparrow-Simpson (1887)

1: I stand amazed in the presence
Of Jesus the Nazarene,
And wonder how He could love me,
A sinner, condemned, unclean.
2: For me it was in the garden
He prayed, "Not My will, but Thine."
He had no tears for His own griefs,
But sweat drops of blood for mine.
3: In pity angels beheld Him
And came from the world of light
To comfort Him in the sorrows
He bore for my soul that night.
4: He took my sins and my sorrows;
He made them His very own.
He bore the burden to Calv'ry
And suffered and died alone.
5: When with the ransomed in glory
His face I at last shall see,
'Twill be my joy through the ages
To sing of His love for me.
chorus: How marvelous! How wonderful!
And my song shall ever be:
How marvelous! How wonderful
Is my Savior's love for me!

244.title: I Stand Amazed
244.credits:
Tune: MY SAVIOR'S LOVE • Charles H. Gabriel (1905)
Hymn: Charles H. Gabriel (1905)

1: Holy words long preserved
For our walk in this world;
They resound with God's own heart;
O let the ancient words impart.
Words of life, words of hope
Give us strength, help us cope;
In this world, where'er we roam,
Ancient words will guide us home.
2: Holy words of our faith,
Handed down to this age,
Came to us through sacrifice;
O heed the faithful words of Christ!
Martyrs' blood stains each page;
They have died for this faith.
Hear them cry through the years
Heed these words and hold them dear
chorus: Ancient words, ever true,
Changing me and changing you.
We have come with open hearts;
O let the ancient words impart.

275.title: Ancient Words
275.credits:
© 2001 Integrity’s Hosanna! Music (admin. by EMI Christian Music Group)
Tune: Lynn DeShazo (2001); arr. The ZOE Group (2003)
Hymn: Lynn DeShazo (2001)

esp.title: Palabras Antiguas
esp.credits:
© 2001 Integrity’s Hosanna! Music (admin. by EMI Christian Music Group)
Música: Lynn DeShazo (2001); arr. The ZOE Group (2003)
Palabras: Lynn DeShazo (2001)

1: Speak, O Lord, as we come to You
To receive the food of Your holy word.
Take Your truth; plant it deep in us;
Shape and fashion us in Your likeness
That the light of Christ might be seen today
In our acts of love and our deeds of faith.
Speak, O Lord and fulfill in us
All Your purposes for Your glory.
2: Teach us Lord, full obedience,
Holy reverence, true humility.
Test our thoughts and our attitudes
In the radiance of Your purity.
Cause our faith to rise; cause our eyes to see
Your majestic love and authority.
Words of pow'r that can never fail,
Let their truth prevail over unbelief.
3: Speak, O Lord, and renew our minds;
Help us grasp the heights of Your plans for us,
Truths unchanged from the dawn of time,
That will echo down through eternity.
And by grace we'll stand on Your promises;
And by faith we'll walk as You walk with us.
Speak, O Lord, till Your church is built
And the earth is filled with Your glory.

277.title: Speak, O Lord
277.credits:
© 2005 Thankyou Music (admin. by EMI Christian Music Group)
Tune: SPEAK O LORD • Keith Getty (2005); Stuart Townend (2005); arr. Charles L. Willis (2011)
Hymn: Keith Getty (2005); Stuart Townend (2005)

esp.title: Habla, Oh Dios
esp.credits:
© 2005 Thankyou Music (admin. by EMI Christian Music Group)
Música: SPEAK O LORD • Keith Getty (2005); Stuart Townend (2005); arr. Charles L. Willis (2011)
Palabras: Keith Getty (2005); Stuart Townend (2005)

1: Break Thou the bread of life,
Dear Lord, to me,
As Thou didst break the loaves
Beside the sea;
Beyond the sacred page
I seek Thee, Lord;
My spirit pants for Thee,
O living Word!
2: Bless Thou the truth, dear Lord,
To me, to me,
As Thou didst bless the bread
By Galilee;
Then shall all bondage cease,
All fetters fall,
And I shall find my peace,
My all in all.
3: Thou art the bread of life,
O Lord, to me,
Thy holy word the truth
That saveth me;
Give me to eat and live
With Thee above;
Teach me to love Thy truth,
For Thou art love.

282.title: Break Thou the Bread of Life
282.credits:
Tune: BREAD OF LIFE • William F. Sherwin (1877)
Hymn: Mary A. Lathbury (1877); st. 3 Alexander Groves (1913)

1: Oh, how great the salvation in Jesus,
That the prophets of old testified!
Even angels desire its revealing.
Praise and glory to Christ crucified!
2: As a lamb that was led to the slaughter,
Not a threat, not a word did You say.
Help me, Lord, when my heart fills with anger.
Your example I will not betray.
3: I've been saved by Your word that is living,
To a love that is fervently pure.
Precious blood from Your cross brings redemption
With a hope that is living and sure.
chorus: You were threatened for me;
You were slandered for me;
Every thorn, every nail, every tear was for me.
Sweet the thought that my soul may be mended and whole
By my Lord who was broken for me.

285.title: Mended and Whole
285.credits:
© 1986 Donald M. Alexander
Tune: Donald M. Alexander (1985); arr. R. J. Stevens (1987)
Hymn: Donald M. Alexander (1985)

1: What can wash away my sin?
Nothing but the blood of Jesus.
What can make me whole again?
Nothing but the blood of Jesus.
2: For my pardon, this I see:
Nothing but the blood of Jesus.
For my cleansing this my plea:
Nothing but the blood of Jesus.
3: Nothing can for sin atone,
Nothing but the blood of Jesus,
Naught of good that I have done,
Nothing but the blood of Jesus.
4: This is all my hope and peace:
Nothing but the blood of Jesus.
This is all my righteousness:
Nothing but the blood of Jesus.
chorus: Oh! precious is the flow
That makes me white as snow;
No other fount I know,
Nothing but the blood of Jesus.

291.title: Nothing but the Blood
291.credits:
Tune: PLAINFIELD • Robert Lowry (1876)
Hymn: Robert Lowry (1876)

1: Let the weak say, "I am strong";
Let the poor say, "I am rich";
Let the blind say, "I can see";
It's what the Lord has done in me.
2: In the river I will wade;
There my sins are washed away
By the heaven's mercy stream
Of the Savior's love for me.
3: I will rise from waters deep
To the saving arms of God.
I will sing salvation songs:
Jesus Christ has set me free.
chorus: Hosanna,
Hosanna To the Lamb that was slain;
Hosanna,
Hosanna: Jesus died and rose again.

297.title: What the Lord Has Done in Me
297.credits:
© 1998, arr. 2010 Reuben Morgan and Hillsong Pub. (admin. by EMI Christian Music Group, Inc.)
Tune: Reuben Morgan (1998)
Hymn: Reuben Morgan (1998)

esp.title: Diga El Débil, “Fuerte Soy”
esp.credits:
© 1998, arr. 2010 Reuben Morgan and Hillsong Pub. (admin. by EMI Christian Music Group, Inc.)
Musica: Reuben Morgan (1998)
Palabras: Reuben Morgan (1998)

1: Jesus, and shall it ever be,
A mortal man ashamed of Thee?
Ashamed of Thee, whom angels praise,
Whose glories shine through endless days?
2: Ashamed of Jesus! sooner far
Let evening blush to own a star;
He sheds the beams of light divine
O'er this benighted soul of mine.
3: Ashamed of Jesus! yes, I may,
When I've no guilt to wash away,
No tears to wipe, no good to crave,
No fears to quell, no soul to save.
4: Till then, nor is my boasting vain,
Till then I boast a savior slain;
And O, may this my glory be,
That Christ is not ashamed of me.
chorus: And O, may this my glory be,
My glory be, my glory be,
That Christ is not ashamed of me,
Is not ashamed of me.

300.title: May This My Glory Be
300.credits:
© 1972 Tillit S. Teddlie
Tune: Tillit S. Teddlie (1972)
Hymn: Joseph Grigg (1765); cho. Tillit S. Teddlie (1972)

1: Before the throne of God above,
I have a strong, a perfect plea,
A great high priest whose name is love,
Who ever lives and pleads for me.
My name is graven on His hands;
My name is written on His heart.
I know that while in heav'n He stands
No tongue can bid me thence depart,
No tongue can bid me thence depart.
2: Behold Him there, the risen Lamb,
My perfect, spotless righteousness,
The great, unchangeable I AM,
The King of glory and of grace.
Now one with Him, I cannot die.
My soul is purchased by His blood.
My life is hid with Christ on high,
With Christ my Savior and my God,
With Christ my Savior and my God.

304.title: Before the Throne of God Above
304.credits:
Arr. © 1997 Sovereign Grace Worship (admin. by EMI Christian Music Group)
Tune: Vicki Cook (1997)
Hymn: Charitie Lees Bancroft (1863); arr. Vicki Cook (1997)

1: In Christ alone my hope is found;
He is my light, my strength, my song;
This cornerstone, this solid ground,
Firm through the fiercest drought and storm.
What heights of love, what depths of peace,
When fears are stilled, when strivings cease!
My comforter, my all in all -
Here in the love of Christ I stand.
2: In Christ alone, who took on flesh,
Fullness of God in helpless babe!
This gift of love and righteousness,
Scorned by the ones He came to save.
Till on that cross, as Jesus died,
The wrath of God was satisfied;
For every sin on Him was laid -
Here in the death of Christ I live.
3: There in the ground His body lay,
Light of the world by darkness slain;
Then bursting forth in glorious day,
Up from the grave He rose again!
And as He stands in victory,
Sin's curse has lost its grip on me;
For I am His and He is mine -
Bought with the precious blood of Christ.
4: No guilt in life, no fear in death -
This is the pow'r of Christ in me;
From life's first cry to final breath,
Jesus commands my destiny.
No pow'r of hell, no scheme of man
Can ever pluck me from His hand;
Till He returns or calls me home -
Here in the pow'r of Christ I'll stand.

306.title: In Christ Alone
306.credits:
© 2001 Thankyou Music (admin. by EMI Christian Music Publishing)
Tune: Keith Getty (2001); Stuart Townend (2001); arr. D. J. Bulls
Hymn: Keith Getty (2001); Stuart Townend (2001)

esp.title: Firme Estoy En Jesús
esp.credits:
© 2001 Thankyou Music (admin. by EMI Christian Music Publishing)
Musica: Keith Getty (2001); Stuart Townend (2001); arr. D. J. Bulls
Palabras: Keith Getty (2001); Stuart Townend (2001)

1: Son of God, eternal Savior,
Source of life and truth and grace,
Son of Man, whose birth incarnate
Hallows all our human race,
You, our Head, enthroned in glory,
For the church will ever plead,
Fill us with Your love and pity,
Heal our wrongs and help our need.
2: Lord, as You have lived for others,
So may we for others live;
Freely have Your gifts been granted,
Freely may Your servants give.
Is there want, or pain, or sorrow?
Make us all the burden share.
Are there spirits crushed and broken?
Teach us, Lord, to soothe their care.
3: Bind us all as one together
In Your church's sacred fold,
Weak and healthy, poor and wealthy,
Sad and joyful, young and old.
As You pray and as You will it -
That Your people should be one -
Grant our hope to be united,
Here on earth Your will be done.

341.title: Son of God, Eternal Savior
341.credits:
Tune: HYFRYDOL • Rowland H. Prichard (1830); composite arr. 
Hymn: Somerset C. Lowry (1894)

esp.title: Salvador Eterno, Hijo de Dios
esp.credits:
Música por: HYFRYDOL • Rowland H. Prichard (1830); composite arr. 
Palabras por: Somerset C. Lowry (1894)

1: Dear Lord, we break the bread in memory
Of that great sacrifice on Calvary.
This we do each Lord's day as Christ has said.
Bless all disciples now who break the bread.
2: Bless Thou the cup, dear Lord, to us this day.
May we with hearts prepared His word obey;
We now His death proclaim, in His own way;
Until He comes again, we keep this day.
3: Our Savior now doth reign in heav'n above;
Death's pow'r He overcame, such matchless love!
To heav'n He did ascend; there He's enthroned;
He is our dearest friend, for us atoned.

357.title: The Breaking of Bread
357.credits:
Words © 1956 Ellis J. Crum (admin. by Sacred Selections)
Tune: BREAD OF LIFE • William F. Sherwin (1877)
Hymn: Ellis J. Crum (1956)

esp.title: El Partir Del Pan
esp.credits:
Palabras © 1956 Ellis J. Crum (admin. by Sacred Selections)
Música: BREAD OF LIFE • William F. Sherwin (1877)
Himno: Ellis J. Crum (1956)

1: How deep the Father's love for us,
How vast beyond all measure,
That He should give His only Son
To make a wretch His treasure.
How great the pain of searing loss:
The Father turns His face away,
As wounds which mar the Chosen One
Bring many sons to glory.
2: Behold the man upon a cross,
My sin upon His shoulders;
Ashamed, I hear my mocking voice
Call out among the scoffers.
It was my sin that held Him there
Until it was accomplished;
His dying breath has brought me life -
I know that it is finished.
3: I will not boast in anything,
No gifts, no pow'r, no wisdom;
But I will boast in Jesus Christ,
His death and resurrection.
Why should I gain from His reward?
I cannot give an answer,
But this I know with all my heart,
His wounds have paid my ransom.

448.title: How Deep the Father's Love
448.credits:
© 1995 Thankyou Music (admin. by EMI Christian Music Publishing)
Tune: TOWNEND • Stuart Townend (1995)
Hymn: Stuart Townend (1995)

esp.title: Profundo Es El Amor De Dios
esp.credits:
© 1995 Thankyou Music (admin. by EMI Christian Music Publishing)
Musica: TOWNEND • Stuart Townend (1995)
Palabras: Stuart Townend (1995)

1: And can it be that I should gain
An interest in the Savior's blood?
Died He for me who caused His pain,
For me who scorned His perfect love?
2: You left Your Father's throne above -
So free and infinite Your grace -
Emptied Yourself of all but love,
And bled for Adam's helpless race.
3: Boldly I come before Your throne,
To claim Your mercy immense and free.
No greater love will e'er be known,
For, O my God, it found out me.
chorus: Amazing love! How can it be
That You, my God, should die for me?
Amazing love! How can it be
That You, my God, should die for me?

451.title: And Can It Be?
451.credits:
© 1988 Bridge Building Music/Organon Key Music (admin. by Brentwood-Benson Music Publishing, Inc.)
Tune: Bob Kauflin (1988); arr. Matthew L. Harber (2011)
Hymn: Charles Wesley (1738); arr. Bob Kauflin (1988)

esp.title: ¿Y Cómo Es?
esp.credits:
© 1988 Bridge Building Music/Organon Key Music (admin. by Brentwood-Benson Music Publishing, Inc.)
Musica: Bob Kauflin (1988); arr. Matthew L. Harber (2011)
Palabras: Charles Wesley (1738); arr. Bob Kauflin (1988)

1: Must I live a life of sinning
Just to know God will forgive me?
Must I then be brokenhearted
Just to know that He can cheer me?
2: Must I be in heaven with Him
Just to know the Lord is reigning?
Must I see His acts of power
Just to know that He is with me?
3: Must my prayers be loudly spoken
Just to know the Father hears me?
Must I die with no one knowing
Just to know the Father sees me?
4: Must I see the King leave heaven
Just to keep my hopeful yearning?
Must I watch with perfect vision
Just to know that He's returning?
chorus: Praise the Lord!  I am forgiven!
And my Father up in heaven
Knows and hears and will be with me;
Praise the Lord!  I am His child!

455.title: I Am His Child
455.credits:
© 1986 Donald M. Alexander
Tune: Donald M. Alexander (1985); arr. R. J. Stevens (1987)
Hymn: Donald M. Alexander (1985)

1: Take my life, O Father, mold it
In obedience to Thy will;
And as rip'ning years unfold it,
Keep it true and childlike still.
2: Father, keep it pure and holy,
Strong and brave, yet free from strife,
Turning from the paths unholy
Of a vain or sinful life.
3: Ever let Thy might surround it,
Girding well the inner mind,
Till the cords of love have bound it,
Father, wholly unto Thine.

478.title: Take My Life, O Father, Mold It
478.credits:
Tune: TALMAR • Isaac B. Woodbury (1845)
Hymn: Bartol's Hymns for the Sanctuary (1849); arr. Hymn Tune and Service Book (1879)

1: Seek ye first the kingdom of God
And His righteousness,
And all these things shall be added unto you.
Allelu-, Alleluia!
2: Ask and it shall be given unto you;
Seek and ye shall find;
Knock and the door shall be opened unto you.
Allelu-, Alleluia!
3: Man shall not live by bread alone,
But by every word
That proceeds from the mouth of God.
Allelu-, Alleluia!

483.title: Seek Ye First
483.credits:
© 1972 Maranatha! Music (admin. by Brentwood-Benson Music Publishing, Inc.)
Tune: LAFFERTY • Karen Lafferty (1972)
Hymn: arr. Karen Lafferty (1972)

1: We walk by faith and not by sight;
No gracious words we hear
From Him who spoke as man ne'er spoke,
But we believe Him near.
2: We may not touch His hands and side,
Nor follow where He trod;
But in His promise we rejoice,
And cry, "My Lord and God!"
3: Help then, O Lord, our unbelief;
And may our faith abound,
To call on You when You are near
And seek where You are found.
4: That, when our life of faith is done,
In realms of clearer light
We may behold You as You are,
With full and endless sight.

494.title: We Walk by Faith
494.credits:
Tune: IRISH • Powell's Collection of Hymns and Sacred Poems (1749)
Hymn: Henry Alford (1844)

1: Be strong and courageous, you hosts of the LORD!
Rise up for the battle, and gird on your swords.
The land of His promise is yours now by right;
Take all He has given; go forth to the fight!
2: Be strong and courageous! Through God you will win,
Though great be the armies of error and sin.
Your Captain will lead you to conquer the land;
His arm cannot fail you, and they cannot stand.
3: Be strong and courageous! Consider His law;
Regard it with rev'rence; perform it with awe.
Be careful to follow all God has decreed,
For then He will bless you, and you will succeed.
chorus: Be strong and courageous, and conquer the foe;
The LORD God is with you wherever you go!

514.title: Be Strong and Courageous
514.credits:
© 2001 Glenda B. Schales
Tune: Glenda B. Schales (2001)
Hymn: M. W. Bassford (2001)

1: O Church, arise and put your armor on;
Hear the call of Christ our captain.
For now the weak can say that they are strong
In the strength that God has given.
With shield of faith and belt of truth
We'll stand against the devil's lies,
An army bold, whose battle-cry is love,
Reaching out to those in darkness.
2: Our call to war - to love the captive soul,
But to rage against the captor;
And with the sword that makes the wounded whole,
We will fight with faith and valor.
When faced with trials on every side,
We know the outcome is secure,
And Christ will have the prize for which He died:
An inheritance of nations.
3: Come see the cross, where love and mercy meet
As the Son of God is stricken;
Then see His foes lie crushed beneath His feet,
For the Conqueror has risen!
And as the stone is rolled away
And Christ emerges from the grave,
This vict'ry march continues till the day
Every eye and heart shall see Him.
4: So Spirit come, put strength in every stride,
Give grace for every hurdle,
That we may run with faith to win the prize
Of a servant good and faithful.
As saints of old still line the way,
Re-telling triumphs of His grace,
We hear their calls and hunger for the day
When with Christ we stand in glory.

524.title: O Church, Arise
524.credits:
© 2005 Thankyou Music (admin. by EMI Christian Music Publishing)
Tune: Keith Getty (2005); Stuart Townend (2005); arr. Charles L. Willis (2011)
Hymn: Keith Getty (2005); Stuart Townend (2005)

1: Faith of our fathers, living still,
In spite of dungeon, fire and sword -
Oh, how our hearts beat high with joy
Whenever we hear that glorious word!
2: Faith of our fathers, we will strive
To win all nations unto thee;
And through the truth that comes from God,
We all shall then be truly free.
3: Our fathers, chained in prisons dark,
Were still in heart and conscience free;
How sweet would be their children's fate,
If they, like them, could die for thee!
4: Faith of our fathers, we will love
Both friend and foe in all our strife,
And preach thee, too, as love knows how,
By kindly words and virtuous life.
chorus: Faith of our fathers, holy faith!
We will be true to thee till death.

531.title: Faith of Our Fathers
531.credits:
Tune: ST. CATHERINE • Henri F. Hemy (1864); arr. James G. Walton (1874)
Hymn: Frederick W. Faber (1849)

esp.title: La Fe Patriarca
esp.credits:
Música: ST. CATHERINE • Henri F. Hemy (1864); arr. James G. Walton (1874)
Palabras: Frederick W. Faber (1849)

1: Make me a servant,
Just like Your Son,
For He was a servant;
Please make me one.
Make me a servant;
Do what You must do
To make me a servant;
Make me like You.
2: Make me a servant;
Take all my pride,
For I would be lowly,
Humble inside,
Giving to others
With all that I do
In love for my brother;
Make me like You.
3: Make me a servant,
Filled by Your might,
And may all my labors
Shine with Your light.
Show me Your footsteps
And what I should do;
For now and forever,
Make me like You.

591.title: Servant Song
591.credits:
© 1978 Communiqué Music, Inc.; st. 2-3 © 2000 Tim Jennings
Tune: MAKE ME LIKE YOU • Jimmy Owens (1978); Carol Owens (1978); arr. Richard L. Morrison (2000)
Hymn: Jimmy Owens (1978); Carol Owens (1978); arr. Tim Jennings (2000); st. 2-3 M. W. Bassford (2000)

esp.title: Hazme Un Siervo
esp.credits: © 1978 Communiqué Music, Inc.; st. 2-3 © 2000 Tim JenningsMúsica: MAKE ME LIKE YOU • Jimmy Owens (1978); Carol Owens (1978); arr. Richard L. Morrison (2000)Palabras: Jimmy Owens (1978); Carol Owens (1978); arr. Tim Jennings (2000); st. 2-3 M. W. Bassford (2000)

1: Jesus, draw me ever nearer,
As I labor through the storm.
You have called me to this passage,
And I'll follow, though I'm worn.
2: Jesus, guide me through the tempest;
Keep my spirit staid and sure.
When the midnight meets the morning,
Let me love You even more.
3: Let the treasures of the trial
Form within me as I go,
At the end of this long passage,
Let me leave them at Your throne.
chorus: May this journey bring a blessing;
May I rise on wings of faith;
And at the end of my heart's testing,
With Your likeness let me wake.

650.title: Jesus, Draw Me Ever Nearer
650.credits:
music © 2001 Modern M Music (admin. by Music Services)
Words © 2001 Thankyou Music (admin. by EMI Christian Music Publishing);
Tune: Keith Getty (2001); arr. Charles L. Willis (2011)
Hymn: Margaret Becker (2001)

esp.title: Jesús Acércame A Ti
esp.credits:
Musica © 2001 Modern M Music (admin. by Music Services)
Palabras © 2001 Thankyou Music (admin. by EMI Christian Music Publishing);
Musica: Keith Getty (2001); arr. Charles L. Willis (2011)
Palabras: Margaret Becker (2001)

1: On Jordan's stormy banks I stand
And cast a wishful eye
To Canaan's fair and happy land,
Where my possessions lie.
2: O'er all those wide extended plains
Shines one eternal day;
There God the Sun forever reigns
And scatters night away.
3: When I shall reach that happy place
And be forever blest?
When shall I see my Father's face
And in His bosom rest?
chorus: I am bound for the promised land,
I am bound for the promised land.
O who will come and go with me?
I am bound for the promised land.

699.title: I Am Bound for the Promised Land (MCINTOSH)
699.credits:
I Am Bound for the Promised Land
Tune: PROMISED LAND • Walker's Southern Harmony (1835); arr. Rigdon M. McIntosh (1874)
Hymn: Samuel Stennett (1787); cho. Rigdon M. McIntosh (1874)

esp.title: Estando A Orillas Del Jordan
esp.credits:
A Tierra Prometida Voy
Musica: PROMISED LAND • Walker's Southern Harmony (1835); arr. Rigdon M. McIntosh (1874)
Palabras: Samuel Stennett (1787); cho. Rigdon M. McIntosh (1874)

1: When life on earth for me is past,
O Savior, take my hand.
Lord, grant that I might firmly stand
Upon the golden strand.
2: When I shall cease to walk life's road,
Let me come home to Thee;
Take me into Thy blest abode
To dwell eternally.
3: When all my labors are complete,
And I can work no more;
O Savior, guide my weary feet
Through death to heaven's door.
chorus: For soon my journey here must end,
My earthly life must fade.
Lord, grant that I might then ascend,
Immortally arrayed.

708.title: Immortally Arrayed
708.credits:
© 1986 Harry Presley and R. J. Stevens
Tune: R. J. Stevens (1986)
Hymn: Harry Presley (1986)

1: This world is not my home; I'm just a-passing through.
My treasures are laid up somewhere beyond the blue.
The angels beckon me from heaven's open door,
And I can't feel at home in this world anymore.
2: They're all expecting me, and that's one thing I know;
My Savior pardoned me, and now I onward go.
I know He'll take me through though I am weak and poor,
And I can't feel at home in this world anymore.
3: Just up in gloryland we'll live eternally,
The saints on every hand are shouting victory,
Their songs of sweetest praise drift back from heaven's shore,
And I can't feel at home in this world anymore.
chorus: O Lord, You know I have no friend like You.
If heaven's not my home, then Lord what will I do?
The angels beckon me from heaven's open door,
And I can't feel at home in this world anymore.

715.title: This World Is Not My Home
715.credits:
Arr. © 1937, renewal 1965 Albert E. Brumley
Tune: Brumley's Radio Favorites (1937); arr. Albert E. Brumley (1937)
Hymn: Davis' Joyful Meeting in Glory (1919); arr. Albert E. Brumley (1937)

1: Some glad morning when this life is o'er,
I'll fly away;
To a home on God's celestial shore,
I'll fly away.
2: When the shadows of this life have grown,
I'll fly away;
Like a bird from prison bars has flown,
I'll fly away.
3: Just a few more weary days and then
I'll fly away;
To a land where joys shall never end,
I'll fly away.
chorus: I'll fly away, O glory,
I'll fly away;
When I die, hallelujah, by and by,
I'll fly away.

740.title: I'll Fly Away
740.credits:
Hymn: Albert E. Brumley (1932)
Tune: Albert E. Brumley (1932)
© 1932 Hartford Music Co., renewal 1960 Albert E. Brumley and Sons

101.title: Just As I Am (I Come Broken)

esp.title: Tal Como Soy (Soy Culpable)

1:
1. 
O grant us light, that we may know
The wisdom Thou alone canst give;
That truth may guide wherever we go,
And virtue bless wherever we live.
2:
2. 
O grant us light, that we may see
Where error lurks in human lore,
And turn our doubting minds to Thee,
And love Thy simple Word the more.
3:
3. 
O grant us light, that we may learn 
How dead is life from Thee apart,
How sure is joy for all who turn
To thee an undivided heart.

102.title: O Grant Us Light
102.credits:
Words: Lawrence Tuttiett
Music: Louis Bourgeois

esp.title: Oh Dadnos Luz
esp.credits:
Palabras: Lawrence Tuttiett
Musica: Louis Bourgeois

1:
Who has held the oceans in His hands?
Who has numbered ev'ry grain of sand?
Kings and nations tremble at His voice.
All Creation rises to rejoice.
2:
Who has given counsel to the Lord?
Who can question any of His words?
Who can teach the One who knows all things?
Who can fathom all His wondrous deeds?
Behold our God, seated on His throne.
Come, let us adore Him.
Behold our King, nothing can compare,
come, let us adore Him!
3:
Who has felt the nails upon His hands,
bearing all the guilt of sinful man?
God eternal, humbled to the grave,
Jesus, Savior, risen now to reign!
Behold our God, seated on His throne.
Come, let us adore Him.
Behold our King, nothing can compare,
come, let us adore Him!

103.title: Behold Our God
103.credits:
Words: Jonathan & Meghan Baird
Music: Ryan Baird & Stephen Altrogge

esp.title: Contempla A Dios
esp.credits:
Palabras: Jonathan & Meghan Baird
Musica: Ryan Baird & Stephen Altrogge

1: Jesus, the very thought of Thee
With sweetness fills my breast;
But sweeter far Thy face to see,
And in Thy presence rest.
2: O Jesus, light of all below,
Thou fount of living fire,
Surpassing all the joys we know
And all we can desire.
3: O hope of every contrite heart,
O joy of all the meek,
To those who fall, how kind Thou art!
How good to those who seek!
4: Jesus, our only joy be Thou,
As Thou our prize wilt be;
Jesus, be Thou our glory now,
And through eternity.

104.title: Jesus, the Very Thought of Thee
104.credits:
Hymn: Latin Hymn (1150); tr. Edward Caswall (1849)
Music: Greatorex’s Collection

esp.title: Tan Sólo Con Pensar En Ti
esp.credits:
Palabras: Latin Hymn (1150); tr. Edward Caswall (1849)
Música: Greatorex’s Collection

1: O Lord, I need a mountain to climb on; Just a quiet place to go and know You’re there. O Lord, I need to spend some time with You; Spend the night with You, dear Lord, in prayer. And the greatest Friend you’ll ever find is on a lonely mountain. And the highest high you’ll ever feel is when you kneel to pray. And the brightest light you’ll ever see is when you close your eyes. O Lord, You are my first love; at last I realize. O Lord, You are my first love; at last I realize.
2: O Lord, I tho’t the day would never dawn, When I’d lay my burdens down and walk with You; But this morning as I met the rising sun; I felt, dear Lord, my dreams had all come true. And the greatest Friend you’ll ever find is on a lonely mountain. And the highest high you’ll ever feel is when you kneel to pray. And the brightest light you’ll ever see is when you close your eyes. O Lord, You are my first love; at last I realize. O Lord, You are my first love; at last I realize.

esp.title: La Canción Del Monte
esp.credits: Música y Palabras: Roger Carson

1:
1.
Go, labor on: spend and be spent—
Thy joy to do the Father's will;
It is the way the Master went:
Should not the servant tread it still?

176.title: Go, Labor On
176.credits:
Words: Horatius Bonar/ Arr.: William Kethe
Music: Louis Bourgeois’s Genevan Psalter

1: We walk by faith and not by sight;
No gracious words we hear
From Him who spoke as man ne'er spoke,
But we believe Him near.
2: We may not touch His hands and side,
Nor follow where He trod;
But in His promise we rejoice,
And cry, "My Lord and God!"
3: Help then, O Lord, our unbelief;
And may our faith abound,
To call on You when You are near
And seek where You are found.
4: That, when our life of faith is done,
In realms of clearer light
We may behold You as You are,
With full and endless sight.

494.title: We Walk by Faith
494.credits:
Music by: Carl Gläser / Arr. Lowell Mason
Hymn: Henry Alford (1844)

1:
1. 
Where charity and love prevail
There God is ever found;
Brought here together by Christ's love,
By love are we thus bound.
2:
2. 
With grateful joy and holy fear
His charity we learn;
Let us with heart and mind and soul
Now love Him in return.
3:
3. 
Forgive we now each other's faults
As we our faults confess,
And let us love each other well
In Christian holiness.
4:
4. 
Let strife among us be unknown,
Let all contention cease;
Be His the glory that we see,
Be ours His holy peace.
5:
5. 
Let us recall that in our midst
Dwells God's begotten Son;
As members of His body joined,
We are in Him made one.

759.title: Where Charity and Love Prevail
759.credits:
Words: Latin Hymn / Trans.: Omer Westenforf / Music: John Campbell
Translation Copyright © 1961 by World Library Publications, Inc. All rights reserved. Used by permission.
